text: Welche ZWEI Elemente erscheinen in nahezu jeder Definition von Softwarearchitektur?
text_en: Which TWO elements appear in virtually every definition of software architecture?
explanation: Definitions of software architecture consistently name two core elements: structural building blocks (components, modules) and their relationships/interfaces. Other aspects — deployment topology, programming language, team size — vary by author and context and are not universally included in the definition itself.

choices.text: Bausteine und ihre Schnittstellen
choices.text_en: Building blocks and their interfaces

choices.text: Beziehungen zwischen Bausteinen
choices.text_en: Relationships between building blocks

choices.text: Gewählte Programmiersprache
choices.text_en: Chosen programming language

choices.text: Projektmanagementmethodik
choices.text_en: Project management methodology

choices.text: Laufzeit-Deployment-Topologie
choices.text_en: Runtime deployment topology

choices.text: Teamgröße und -organisation
choices.text_en: Team size and organization

text: Ein neues Teammitglied fragt: 'Was genau ist die Aufgabe der Softwarearchitektur in unserem Projekt?' Welche Antwort beschreibt ihre Rolle am besten?
text_en: A new team member asks: 'What exactly is the job of software architecture on our project?' Which answer BEST captures its role?
explanation: Software architecture defines the significant structural decisions — which building blocks exist, how they interact, and the rationale for those choices. It does not specify every implementation detail (that is left to developers), nor does it replace detailed design documents. The architecture provides the structural framework within which all other decisions are made.

choices.text: Definiert wesentliche Strukturentscheidungen und deren Begründung
choices.text_en: Defines significant structural decisions and their rationale

choices.text: Legt vorab jedes Implementierungsdetail fest
choices.text_en: Specifies every implementation detail in advance

choices.text: Bestimmt die Algorithmenauswahl für jedes Modul
choices.text_en: Determines the algorithm selection for each module

choices.text: Ersetzt die Notwendigkeit detaillierter Entwurfsdokumente
choices.text_en: Replaces the need for detailed design documents

choices.text: Legt Projektbudget und Ressourcenzuteilung fest
choices.text_en: Defines project budget and resource allocation

text: Ordnen Sie jeden Architekturbegriff seiner korrekten Definition zu.
text_en: Match each architectural term to its correct definition.
explanation: These are the core iSAQB vocabulary terms for structural elements of a software architecture. A Component is a self-contained part with a defined interface; a Connector links components; a System is the complete product; a Subsystem is a major functional grouping that may contain multiple components; a Pattern is a named, reusable solution to a recurring design problem.

choices.text: Ein wesentlicher Teil des Systems mit einer klar definierten Schnittstelle
choices.text_en: A major part of the system with a well-defined interface

choices.text: Ein Verbindungs- oder Interaktionsmechanismus zur Verknüpfung zweier Komponenten
choices.text_en: A connection or interaction mechanism linking two components

choices.text: Das vollständige Softwareprodukt, das erstellt oder analysiert wird
choices.text_en: The complete software product being built or analyzed

choices.text: Ein wesentlicher funktionaler Teil, der mehrere Komponenten enthalten kann
choices.text_en: A major functional part that may contain multiple components

choices.text: Eine benannte, wiederverwendbare Lösung für ein wiederkehrendes Entwurfsproblem
choices.text_en: A named, reusable solution to a recurring design problem

text: Die Definition von Grady Booch besagt, dass Architektur 'die Menge bedeutsamer Entscheidungen über die Organisation eines Softwaresystems' ist. Was macht eine Entscheidung 'bedeutsam'?
text_en: The Grady Booch definition states that architecture is 'the set of significant decisions about the organization of a software system'. What makes a decision 'significant'?
explanation: A decision is architecturally significant when it is hard to reverse and has wide impact across the system or strongly influences quality attributes. Trivial or easily reversible decisions — such as a variable name or a local algorithm choice — are not architectural. The cost of changing the decision later is a key indicator of its significance.

choices.text: Schwer rückgängig zu machen und hat weitreichende systemweite Auswirkungen
choices.text_en: Hard to reverse and has wide system-wide impact

choices.text: Wurde vom ranghöchsten Teammitglied getroffen
choices.text_en: Made by the most senior team member

choices.text: In einem formalen Spezifikationsdokument dokumentiert
choices.text_en: Documented in a formal specification document

choices.text: Explizit im Vertrag des Auftraggebers gefordert
choices.text_en: Required explicitly by the client's contract

choices.text: Betrifft nur ein einzelnes, isoliertes Modul
choices.text_en: Affects only a single, isolated module

text: Welche DREI Eigenschaften werden typischerweise durch Architekturentscheidungen beeinflusst?
text_en: Which THREE properties are typically affected by architectural decisions?
explanation: Architectural decisions shape: (1) the structure of the system — how building blocks are organized and relate; (2) quality attributes such as performance, security, and maintainability; and (3) constraints on future development — which options remain open and which are foreclosed. Coding style conventions and UI color choices are within a single component and do not constitute architectural decisions.

choices.text: Systemstruktur und Komponentenorganisation
choices.text_en: System structure and component organization

choices.text: Qualitätsmerkmale (Performance, Sicherheit, Wartbarkeit)
choices.text_en: Quality attributes (performance, security, maintainability)

choices.text: Randbedingungen für zukünftige technische Entscheidungen
choices.text_en: Constraints on future technical decisions

choices.text: Coding-Stil-Konventionen innerhalb eines einzelnen Services
choices.text_en: Coding style conventions within a single service

choices.text: Farbpalette und visuelles Branding der Benutzeroberfläche
choices.text_en: User interface color palette and visual branding

choices.text: Testbenennungskonventionen des QA-Teams
choices.text_en: Test naming conventions used by the QA team

text: Welche ZWEI Eigenschaften machen eine Architekturentscheidung BEDEUTSAM (architekturrelevant)?
text_en: Which TWO characteristics make an architecture decision SIGNIFICANT (architecturally relevant)?
explanation: Significant architectural decisions share two key traits: they are difficult or costly to reverse (high change cost), and they affect multiple quality attributes or components across the system (broad impact). Documentation, committee approval, and timing are process characteristics, not what makes a decision architecturally significant.

choices.text: Schwierig oder kostspielig, später rückgängig zu machen
choices.text_en: Difficult or costly to reverse later

choices.text: Beeinflusst mehrere Qualitätsmerkmale oder Komponenten
choices.text_en: Affects multiple quality attributes or components

choices.text: Genehmigt durch ein Architektur-Review-Gremium
choices.text_en: Approved by an architecture review board

choices.text_en: Documented in the project's architecture wiki

choices.text: In der frühesten Phase des Projekts getroffen
choices.text_en: Made during the earliest stage of the project

text: Welche VIER sind grundlegende Vorteile einer EXPLIZITEN Softwarearchitektur?
text_en: Which FOUR are fundamental benefits of having an EXPLICIT software architecture?
explanation: Explicit architecture provides: (1) a common communication basis so all stakeholders share a mental model; (2) guidance for implementation decisions within the constraints set; (3) a basis for fulfilling quality requirements by making them visible and traceable; and (4) a foundation for identifying and managing technical risks early. The two incorrect options describe desirable but unrelated outcomes that architecture does not directly guarantee.

choices.text: Gemeinsame Kommunikationsgrundlage für alle Stakeholder
choices.text_en: Common communication basis for all stakeholders

choices.text: Orientierung für Implementierungsentscheidungen
choices.text_en: Guidance for implementation decisions

choices.text: Grundlage zur Erfüllung von Qualitätsanforderungen
choices.text_en: Basis for fulfilling quality requirements

choices.text: Fundament zur Identifikation und zum Management von Risiken

choices.text: Garantierte termingerechte Lieferung des Projekts
choices.text_en: Guaranteed on-time delivery of the project

choices.text: Reduzierung der insgesamt benötigten Entwickleranzahl
choices.text_en: Reduction in the total number of developers needed

text: Klassifizieren Sie jede Entscheidung als ARCHITEKTURENTSCHEIDUNG oder als IMPLEMENTIERUNGSENTSCHEIDUNG.
text_en: Classify each decision as an ARCHITECTURAL decision or an IMPLEMENTATION decision.
explanation: Architectural decisions define structure, interfaces, and quality-affecting choices that are hard to reverse and broadly impact the system. Implementation decisions are local, easily reversible choices within a component. REST vs. messaging affects all inter-service communication (architectural); database technology affects the entire persistence layer (architectural); a sorting algorithm inside one module is easily swapped without structural impact (implementation); variable naming is purely local style (implementation).

choices.text: Wahl zwischen REST und Messaging für die Kommunikation zwischen Services
choices.text_en: Choosing REST vs. messaging for inter-service communication

choices.text: Zerlegung des Systems in Schichten (UI, Fachlogik, Daten)
choices.text_en: Decomposing the system into layers (UI, business, data)

choices.text: Wahl eines Sortieralgorithmus innerhalb eines einzelnen Moduls
choices.text_en: Choosing a sorting algorithm inside a single module

choices.text: Benennung einer lokalen Variable als 'index' vs. 'i'
choices.text_en: Naming a local variable 'index' vs. 'i'

choices.text: Auswahl der Datenbanktechnologie für das gesamte System
choices.text_en: Selecting the database technology for the entire system

text: Klassifiziere jede Entscheidung als Architekturentscheidung oder als Implementierungsentscheidung.
text_en: Classify each decision as an architectural decision or an implementation decision.
explanation: Architectural decisions shape the overall structure of the system and are hard to reverse — subsystem decomposition, interface definitions, and deployment topology all qualify. Decisions about internals of a single module or class (variable naming, algorithm choice within a component) are implementation decisions because they are local and easily reversible without affecting other parts of the system.

choices.text_en: Division of system responsibilities across subsystems

text: Welche DREI Arten von Bausteinen beschreibt das iSAQB-Curriculum?
text_en: Which THREE types of building blocks does the iSAQB curriculum describe?
explanation: The iSAQB curriculum distinguishes three types: white-box building blocks (internals are visible and documented), black-box building blocks (only the interface is visible, internals are hidden), and cross-cutting concepts (a concern that spans multiple building blocks, such as logging or security). 'Gray-box' and 'macro-box' are not iSAQB terms.

choices.text: White-box (Innenleben sichtbar)
choices.text_en: White-box (internals visible)

choices.text: Black-box (nur Schnittstelle sichtbar)
choices.text_en: Black-box (only interface visible)

choices.text: Querschnittskonzept (betrifft mehrere Bausteine)
choices.text_en: Cross-cutting concept (spans multiple blocks)

choices.text: Gray-box (teilweise sichtbares Innenleben)
choices.text_en: Gray-box (partially visible internals)

choices.text: Macro-box (Aggregation auf Systemebene)
choices.text_en: Macro-box (system-level aggregation)

text: Das Zahlungsabwicklungssystem einer Bank ist über acht Jahre ohne bewusstes Design gewachsen. Es existiert keine Architekturdokumentation, und verschiedene Teams haben widersprüchliche Entscheidungen getroffen. Welcher Begriff beschreibt diese Situation?
text_en: A bank's payment processing system has grown over eight years with no deliberate design. No architecture documentation exists and different teams have made conflicting decisions. Which term describes this situation?
explanation: This is implicit architecture — structure that has emerged from accumulated development decisions without intentional design. Every system has an architecture; the question is whether it is explicit (deliberately designed and communicated) or implicit (emerged and undocumented). Making it explicit enables analysis, communication, and intentional improvement. 'Legacy architecture' and 'technical debt' are related but different concepts.

choices.text: Implizite Architektur — aus Entscheidungen ohne explizites Design entstanden
choices.text_en: Implicit architecture — emerged from decisions without explicit design

choices.text: Legacy-Architektur — ein veraltetes, aber absichtliches Design
choices.text_en: Legacy architecture — an outdated but intentional design

choices.text: Zufällige Architektur — ein bekanntes Anti-Pattern im iSAQB-Modell
choices.text_en: Accidental architecture — a known anti-pattern in the iSAQB model

choices.text: Schattenarchitektur — Architektur, die vor Regulierungsbehörden verborgen ist
choices.text_en: Shadow architecture — architecture hidden from regulators

choices.text: Prototyparchitektur — nur für Nicht-Produktionssysteme geeignet
choices.text_en: Prototype architecture — suitable only for non-production systems

text: Welche ZWEI sind typische Verantwortlichkeiten eines Softwarearchitekten?
text_en: Which TWO are typical responsibilities of a software architect?
explanation: Architects make significant design decisions (structural choices with broad impact on quality and structure) and communicate architecture to stakeholders (translating technical decisions into terms each audience understands). Managing the project budget is a project manager's responsibility; conducting all code reviews is a team-shared activity; the architect does not replace the development team.

choices.text: Treffen wesentlicher Entwurfsentscheidungen
choices.text_en: Making significant design decisions

choices.text: Kommunikation der Architektur an Stakeholder
choices.text_en: Communicating architecture to stakeholders

choices.text: Durchführung aller Code-Reviews als Qualitätswächter
choices.text_en: Conducting all code reviews as quality gatekeeper

text: Welche Tätigkeit gehört typischerweise NICHT zu den Aufgaben eines Softwarearchitekten?
text_en: Which activity is NOT typically a responsibility of a software architect?
explanation: Architects focus on structure, quality, and technical communication. Conducting employee performance reviews is a line-management responsibility, not an architectural one. Analyzing quality requirements, selecting architectural patterns, documenting decisions, and communicating design to the team are all genuine architect tasks.

choices.text: Durchführung von Mitarbeiterleistungsbeurteilungen

text: Was bedeutet KONZEPTIONELLE INTEGRITÄT in der Softwarearchitektur?
text_en: What does CONCEPTUAL INTEGRITY mean in software architecture?
explanation: Conceptual integrity, coined by Fred Brooks in 'The Mythical Man-Month', means the architecture follows a coherent and consistent set of design ideas throughout — so the system feels unified and predictable rather than a patchwork of different styles. It does not require a single author or a single language; it requires consistent application of a shared design philosophy. A system without conceptual integrity is harder to understand, extend, and maintain.

choices.text: Das System folgt durchgehend einem kohärenten, konsistenten Satz von Entwurfsideen
choices.text_en: The system follows a coherent, consistent set of design ideas throughout

choices.text: Alle Module verwenden dieselbe Programmiersprache und dasselbe Framework
choices.text_en: All modules use the same programming language and framework

choices.text_en: Every decision was formally reviewed and approved by stakeholders

text: Welche ZWEI Tätigkeiten führt ein Architekt typischerweise durch?
text_en: Which TWO activities does an architect typically perform?
explanation: Two core architect activities are: analyzing requirements to determine their architectural relevance (which requirements force structural decisions?), and creating architectural views and documentation to communicate the design to different audiences. Writing unit tests and setting up CI/CD pipelines are developer and DevOps activities; approving all pull requests is a team process.

choices.text: Analyse von Anforderungen auf architekturelle Relevanz
choices.text_en: Analyzing requirements for architectural relevance

choices.text: Erstellung von Architektursichten und Dokumentation
choices.text_en: Creating architectural views and documentation

choices.text_en: Approving all pull requests before merge

text: Klassifizieren Sie jede Aufgabe als primär eine ARCHITEKTEN-Aufgabe oder eine ENTWICKLER-Aufgabe.
text_en: Classify each task as primarily an ARCHITECT task or a DEVELOPER task.
explanation: Architects focus on structure, system-wide decisions, and communication with stakeholders. Developers work within the architectural constraints to implement features. Deciding inter-service protocols and documenting the building block view are architect tasks; implementing a specific function and writing unit tests for a local algorithm are developer tasks. Trade-off evaluation with stakeholders is an architect responsibility because it requires both technical and communication authority.

choices.text_en: Deciding the inter-service communication protocol

text: Welche ZWEI Qualitäten sollte ein Softwarearchitekt besitzen?
text_en: Which TWO qualities should a software architect possess?
explanation: Architects need technical breadth (understanding a wide range of technology options, patterns, and platforms to make informed trade-off decisions) and strong communication and stakeholder management skills (to explain complex technical choices to diverse audiences and build alignment). Neither alone is sufficient. Deep coding speed in a single language is a developer strength but not an architect differentiator; architects require breadth over depth.

choices.text: Technologische Breite über Technologien und Muster hinweg
choices.text_en: Technical breadth across technologies and patterns

choices.text: Starke Kommunikations- und Stakeholder-Management-Fähigkeiten
choices.text_en: Strong communication and stakeholder management skills

text: Ein Team entscheidet sich, von einer monolithischen zu einer Microservices-Architektur zu wechseln. Welches Merkmal macht dies zu einer ARCHITEKTURENTSCHEIDUNG?
text_en: A team decides to switch from a monolithic to a microservices architecture. Which characteristic makes this an ARCHITECTURAL decision?
explanation: This decision is architecturally significant because it is hard to reverse (requires major restructuring of the entire codebase), affects the entire system structure, and strongly influences multiple quality attributes simultaneously (scalability, deployability, operational complexity, inter-service latency). None of the other options — management approval, documentation, or team size — determine architectural significance; what matters is reversibility and systemic impact.

choices.text: Schwer rückgängig zu machen und betrifft die gesamte Systemstruktur
choices.text_en: Hard to reverse and affects the entire system structure

choices.text: Es erfordert den Input von mehr als drei Entwicklern

text: Ein Architektur-Review für ein Krankenakten-System ist im Gange. Welcher Aspekt sollte die Bewertung als ERSTES leiten?
text_en: An architecture review is underway for a healthcare record system. Which concern should guide the evaluation FIRST?
explanation: The primary evaluation question is always whether the architecture fulfills the required quality attributes — in a healthcare system, this means availability, security, and data integrity above all else. An architecture using the latest technology or conforming to UML standards but failing its quality goals is a failed architecture. Management approval and full team comprehension are process concerns, not architectural fitness criteria.

choices.text: Ob die Architektur die geforderten Qualitätsmerkmale erfüllt
choices.text_en: Whether the architecture fulfills the required quality attributes

choices.text_en: Whether management has formally approved the architecture

choices.text_en: Whether every developer on the team understands every decision

text: Welche Rolle sollte ein Architekt gegenüber dem ENTWICKLUNGSTEAM einnehmen?
text_en: Which role should an architect play regarding the DEVELOPMENT TEAM?
explanation: Architects are most effective as coaches and collaborators — explaining the reasoning behind decisions, actively seeking developer feedback, and adapting where the team surfaces valid concerns. Top-down mandates without discussion reduce developer buy-in and often miss practical implementation issues. Isolation leads to an 'ivory tower architect' anti-pattern where the architecture diverges from what is implementable.

choices.text: Coach und Kollaborateur — Entscheidungen erläutern und Feedback einholen
choices.text_en: Coach and collaborator — explaining decisions and seeking feedback

choices.text: Alleinige Autorität, die Anweisungen ohne Diskussion erteilt
choices.text_en: Sole authority who issues mandates without discussion

choices.text: Isolierter Spezialist, der getrennt vom Entwicklungsteam arbeitet

choices.text: Projektmanager, verantwortlich für Sprint-Planung und Backlog-Pflege
choices.text_en: Project manager responsible for sprint planning and backlog grooming

choices.text: Qualitätswächter, der jeden Code-Commit genehmigen muss
choices.text_en: Quality gatekeeper who must approve every code commit

text: Was ist der PRIMÄRE Zweck einer EXPLIZITEN Softwarearchitektur?
text_en: What is the PRIMARY purpose of having an EXPLICIT software architecture?
explanation: Explicit architecture creates a shared understanding among all stakeholders, guiding consistent implementation decisions and enabling structured communication about the system's structure and quality goals. It does not guarantee delivery timelines, eliminate bugs, or remove the need for testing — those are separate engineering concerns. It provides a foundation that makes the team's work more coherent and intentional.

choices.text: Gemeinsames Verständnis, das konsistente Implementierung leitet
choices.text_en: Shared understanding that guides consistent implementation

choices.text: Garantierte Vermeidung von Integrationsfehlern

choices.text: Reduzierter Bedarf an erfahrenen Entwicklern im Team
choices.text_en: Reduced need for experienced developers on the team

text: Welche ZWEI Qualitäten sollte ein Architekt gegenüber dem ENTWICKLUNGSTEAM zeigen?
text_en: Which TWO qualities should an architect demonstrate toward the DEVELOPMENT TEAM?
explanation: Architects need empathy (understanding the practical implementation challenges developers face) and openness to feedback (the architecture improves through dialogue, not top-down mandates). These qualities build trust and lead to better-implemented architectures. Strict enforcement of every decision without discussion and avoidance of technical conversations both lead to the 'ivory tower architect' anti-pattern.

choices.text: Empathie für Implementierungsherausforderungen
choices.text_en: Empathy for implementation challenges

choices.text: Vermeidung von implementierungsnahen technischen Diskussionen
choices.text_en: Avoidance of implementation-level technical discussions

choices.text: Alleinige Verantwortung für alle querschnittlichen technischen Entscheidungen
choices.text_en: Exclusive ownership of all cross-cutting technical decisions

text: Warum benötigen Organisationen Softwarearchitekten?
text_en: Why do organizations need software architects?
explanation: Modern software systems are too complex for all structural decisions to emerge ad hoc from individual developers. Architects provide direction, ensure quality goals are addressed early, manage technical risk, and align technical work with business goals. The architect role is not about producing more code than others — it is about ensuring the overall structure of the system remains coherent and fit for purpose.

choices.text: Um Komplexität zu beherrschen und technische Entscheidungen mit Geschäftszielen abzustimmen
choices.text_en: To manage complexity and align technical decisions with business goals

choices.text: Um mehr Code-Output als einzelne Entwickler zu produzieren

choices.text_en: To replace project managers in self-organizing agile teams

choices.text: Um die Einhaltung von Coding-Style-Guides teamübergreifend durchzusetzen
choices.text_en: To enforce compliance with coding style guides across teams

text: Klassifizieren Sie jeden Belang als zu ARCHITEKTUR oder zu IMPLEMENTIERUNG gehörend.
text_en: Classify each concern as belonging to ARCHITECTURE or IMPLEMENTATION.
explanation: Architecture concerns are structural, system-wide, or directly affect quality attributes. Implementation concerns are local, internal to a single component, and easily changed without broader impact. Database technology selection affects the entire data layer (architectural); authentication strategy crosses all service boundaries (architectural); inter-service communication style is structural (architectural). Loop constructs and variable naming within a single function are purely local (implementation).

text: Was ist das PRIMÄRE Ziel der Softwarearchitektur?
text_en: What is the PRIMARY goal of software architecture?
explanation: The primary goal of software architecture is to fulfill the system's requirements — both functional (what it must do) and quality-related (how well it must do it) — within the given constraints. Every architectural decision should ultimately serve this goal. Using advanced technology, maximizing services, or minimizing patterns are means to an end, not ends in themselves.

choices.text: Erfüllung von funktionalen und Qualitätsanforderungen im Rahmen der Randbedingungen
choices.text_en: Fulfilling functional and quality requirements within constraints

choices.text: Einsatz der fortschrittlichsten aktuell verfügbaren Technologie
choices.text_en: Adopting the most advanced technology currently available

choices.text_en: Producing the most comprehensive architecture documentation possible

choices.text_en: Minimizing the number of architectural patterns applied

choices.text: Maximierung der Anzahl unabhängig deploybarerer Services
choices.text_en: Maximizing the number of independently deployable services

text: Wenn Architekten sagen 'Architektur ist eine Menge von Entscheidungen', was wird damit betont?
text_en: When architects say 'architecture is a set of decisions', what does this emphasize?
explanation: Viewing architecture as decisions emphasizes that architecture is about choices — each with considered alternatives, explicit rationale, and known consequences — not just boxes-and-arrows diagrams. This framing highlights accountability: decisions can be better or worse, they should be justified, and they can be revisited when context changes. It also prevents architecture from being treated as decoration applied after the fact.

choices.text: Architektur besteht aus Entscheidungen mit Alternativen, Begründung und Konsequenzen
choices.text_en: Architecture consists of choices with alternatives, rationale, and consequences

choices.text: Nur der designierte Architekt darf Entscheidungen treffen

choices.text: Alle Entscheidungen müssen genehmigt werden, bevor eine Implementierung beginnt
choices.text_en: All decisions must be approved before any implementation begins

choices.text_en: Architecture decisions, once made, are permanent and irreversible

choices.text: Visuelle Diagramme sind wichtiger als die dahinter stehende Begründung
choices.text_en: Visual diagrams are more important than the reasoning behind them

text: Was unterscheidet eine ARCHITEKTURRELEVANTE Anforderung von einer gewöhnlichen funktionalen Anforderung?
text_en: What distinguishes an ARCHITECTURALLY RELEVANT requirement from a regular functional requirement?
explanation: Architecturally relevant requirements are those that influence the system structure or significantly affect quality attributes. A requirement to display a customer's name is functional but does not force structural decisions. A requirement for 99.99% availability forces redundancy, failover, and distributed deployment decisions — structural consequences. Author, priority level, or document position do not determine architectural relevance.

choices.text: Sie beeinflusst die Systemstruktur oder hat erhebliche Auswirkungen auf Qualitätsmerkmale
choices.text_en: It influences system structure or significantly impacts quality attributes

choices.text: Sie ist vom Product Owner als 'hohe Priorität' markiert
choices.text_en: It is flagged as 'high priority' by the product owner

choices.text: Sie kann nur mithilfe externer Drittanbieterdienste implementiert werden

text: Welche ZWEI Vorgehensweisen verwendet ein Architekt häufig beim Entwurf eines Systems?
text_en: Which TWO approaches does an architect commonly use when designing a system?
explanation: Architects use top-down decomposition (starting from the overall system and progressively breaking it into subsystems and components) and bottom-up composition (starting from available existing components or libraries and assembling upward). In practice, both are used together — starting top-down reveals structure needs, bottom-up reveals what is already available. Database-first and UI-first are partial starting points, not complete design approaches.

choices.text: Top-down-Zerlegung des Gesamtsystems
choices.text_en: Top-down decomposition of the overall system

choices.text: Bottom-up-Komposition aus vorhandenen Komponenten
choices.text_en: Bottom-up composition from available components

choices.text: Database-first-Design, rein durch das Datenmodell getrieben

choices.text: UI-first-Design, ausschließlich durch Interface-Mockups gesteuert
choices.text_en: UI-first design driven exclusively by interface mockups

choices.text: Opportunistische Auswahl von Patterns aus einem Katalog ohne Analyse
choices.text_en: Opportunistic selection of patterns from a catalog without analysis

text: Was ist im Kontext der Softwarearchitektur ein SYSTEM?
text_en: In the context of software architecture, what is a SYSTEM?
explanation: A system is a purposeful assembly of interacting elements — hardware, software, people, and processes — organized to fulfill a defined set of requirements. The system boundary is a key concept: it defines what is inside (and can be changed by the development team) versus what is outside (external interfaces, third-party systems). A system is not limited to code files or executables.

choices.text: Eine zweckgerichtete Zusammenstellung interagierender Elemente zur Erfüllung einer Menge von Anforderungen
choices.text_en: A purposeful assembly of interacting elements fulfilling a set of requirements

choices.text_en: A single executable artifact deployed on a server

choices.text_en: The production database together with its backup infrastructure

text: Klassifizieren Sie jede Anforderung als QUALITÄTSANFORDERUNG oder als FUNKTIONALE Anforderung.
text_en: Classify each requirement as a QUALITY requirement or a FUNCTIONAL requirement.
explanation: Functional requirements describe specific behaviors or features the system must provide — what it does. Quality requirements (also called non-functional requirements) describe how well the system must perform — performance, security, scalability, availability. The distinction matters architecturally because quality requirements often drive structural decisions that purely functional requirements do not.

choices.text_en: The system must respond within 200ms for 95% of requests

choices.text: Alle ruhenden Daten müssen mit AES-256 verschlüsselt sein
choices.text_en: All data at rest must be encrypted with AES-256

text: Was ist der PRIMÄRE Treiber der Softwarearchitektur?
text_en: What is the PRIMARY driver of software architecture?
explanation: Requirements drive architecture — functional requirements (what the system must do), quality requirements (how well it must do it), and constraints (non-negotiable conditions such as regulatory requirements or existing infrastructure). Without requirements, there is no basis for architectural decisions. Technology preferences, past experience, and budget are inputs to the decision process, but requirements define what the architecture must achieve.

text: Welche der folgenden Anforderungen ist eine FUNKTIONALE Anforderung?
text_en: Which of the following is a FUNCTIONAL requirement?
explanation: Functional requirements describe specific system behaviors or features. 'Users can search for products by name' is a functional requirement — it describes a concrete capability the system must provide. The other options are quality requirements: response time (performance), data encryption (security), system uptime (availability), and user scale (scalability). These describe how well the system must behave, not what it must do.

text: Welche ZWEI sind Beispiele für ARCHITEKTURENTSCHEIDUNGEN (keine Implementierungsentscheidungen)?
text_en: Which TWO are examples of ARCHITECTURAL decisions (not implementation decisions)?
explanation: Architectural decisions affect structure and quality system-wide and are hard to reverse. Choosing event-driven vs. request-response communication defines how all components interact — changing it later requires reworking every integration point. Choosing a layered vs. modular monolith structure defines the overall organization of the entire codebase. By contrast, algorithm choice within one module and class naming are local and easily changed without affecting other parts of the system.

choices.text: Event-driven vs. Request-Response-Kommunikationsstil systemweit
choices.text_en: Event-driven vs. request-response communication style system-wide

text: Welche Aussage beschreibt am GENAUESTEN das Verhältnis von Architektur zur Implementierung?
text_en: Which statement MOST accurately describes how architecture relates to implementation?
explanation: Architecture defines the structural framework and constraints within which implementation occurs. Implementation fills in the details — algorithm choices, data structures, internal class design — within the space the architecture permits. Architecture does not determine every implementation decision, but it constrains the space of valid choices. The two activities are interdependent: implementation feedback can reveal where architectural assumptions were wrong.

choices.text: Architektur definiert Struktur und Randbedingungen; Implementierung füllt die Details innerhalb dieser Randbedingungen aus
choices.text_en: Architecture defines structure and constraints; implementation fills in the details within those constraints

choices.text: Architektur und Implementierung sind zwei völlig getrennte, voneinander unabhängige Tätigkeiten
choices.text_en: Architecture and implementation are two entirely separate, unrelated activities

choices.text: Architektur bestimmt vorab jede Implementierungsentscheidung, bevor die Codierung beginnt
choices.text_en: Architecture pre-determines every implementation decision before coding begins

choices.text_en: In agile projects, implementation always precedes and drives architecture

choices.text: Architektur ist nur für Systeme mit mehr als 50 Entwicklern notwendig
choices.text_en: Architecture is only necessary for systems with more than 50 developers

text: Conways Gesetz besagt: 'Organisationen entwerfen Systeme, die ihre Kommunikationsstrukturen widerspiegeln.' Ein E-Commerce-Unternehmen hat separate Teams für Checkout, Inventar und Kundenkonten, die selten zusammenarbeiten. Welches architektonische Ergebnis sagt Conways Gesetz voraus?
text_en: Conway's Law states: 'Organizations design systems that mirror their communication structures.' An e-commerce company has separate teams for checkout, inventory, and customer accounts that rarely collaborate. What architectural outcome does Conway's Law predict?
explanation: Conway's Law predicts that the system's module boundaries will mirror team boundaries — siloed teams produce siloed components with limited cross-cutting integration. This creates tight coupling within each team's component and awkward, thin interfaces between them. Architects aware of this effect can deliberately use the 'Inverse Conway Maneuver' — reorganizing teams to reflect the desired architecture rather than accepting the architecture that emerges from the current org structure.

choices.text: Checkout, Inventar und Konten werden zu stark abgekapselten Komponenten mit dünnen Integrationsschnittstellen
choices.text_en: Checkout, inventory, and accounts will become tightly siloed components with thin integration interfaces

choices.text_en: The system will naturally converge on a well-integrated, cohesive design

text: Welche DREI Tätigkeiten gehören zu den LAUFENDEN Verantwortlichkeiten eines Architekten (nicht nur beim initialen Entwurf)?
text_en: Which THREE activities belong to an architect's ONGOING responsibilities (not just initial design)?
explanation: Architecture is not a one-time activity. Architects must continuously: (1) monitor conformance — ensuring the implementation stays aligned with architectural decisions; (2) evaluate whether quality goals are still being met as the system evolves; and (3) adapt the architecture as requirements and context change. Hands-on implementation of new features, mandatory pull request approval, and management reporting are not core ongoing architect responsibilities.

choices.text: Überwachung der Übereinstimmung der Implementierung mit Architekturentscheidungen
choices.text_en: Monitoring conformance of the implementation to architectural decisions

choices.text_en: Evaluating whether quality goals are still being met

choices.text: Anpassung der Architektur bei sich ändernden Anforderungen und Rahmenbedingungen
choices.text_en: Adapting the architecture as requirements and context evolve

choices.text_en: Personally implementing all new cross-cutting features

choices.text: Als verpflichtender Genehmiger für jeden Pull Request fungieren
choices.text_en: Serving as mandatory approver for every pull request

choices.text: Erstellung des monatlichen Statusberichts für das Executive Management
choices.text_en: Preparing the monthly status report for executive management

text: Was bedeutet ZWECKEIGNUNG im Kontext der Softwarearchitektur?
text_en: What does FITNESS FOR PURPOSE mean in the context of software architecture?
explanation: Fitness for purpose means the architecture adequately supports the system's intended functions and quality goals within its constraints. An architecture is not inherently good or bad in isolation — it is good if it achieves its purpose. A highly scalable but overly complex architecture for a small internal tool is not fit for purpose. This principle prevents over-engineering and under-engineering alike.

choices.text: Die Architektur erfüllt angemessen die beabsichtigten Funktionen und Qualitätsziele des Systems
choices.text_en: The architecture adequately fulfills the system's intended functions and quality goals

choices.text_en: The architecture uses the most modern and well-regarded patterns

choices.text: Die Architektur ist nach einer anerkannten Standardvorlage dokumentiert
choices.text_en: The architecture is documented using a recognized standard template

choices.text_en: All team members are satisfied with the architectural choices

choices.text_en: The architecture received formal sign-off from management

text: Eine cloudbasierte Gesundheitsplattform muss strenge Datenverschlüsselungsanforderungen (Sicherheit) erfüllen, gleichzeitig aber Echtzeit-Diagnoseanfragen in unter 100ms bedienen (Performance). Das Sicherheitsteam und das klinische Team sind sich über die Priorisierung uneinig. Wie sollte der Architekt mit diesem Konflikt umgehen?
text_en: A cloud-based healthcare platform must meet strict data encryption requirements (security) but also serve real-time diagnostic queries in under 100ms (performance). The security team and the clinical team disagree on prioritization. How should the architect approach this conflict?
explanation: Conflicting quality requirements require explicit trade-off analysis with stakeholder involvement. The architect must surface and quantify the conflict — for example, showing that full encryption at rest adds 30ms to query time — then present options with their respective consequences. Stakeholders (not the architect alone) must decide the business priority. Unilaterally picking one side or delegating to developers avoids the architect's core responsibility.

choices.text: Den Kompromiss quantitativ analysieren und Stakeholder in eine explizite Priorisierungsentscheidung einbeziehen
choices.text_en: Analyze the trade-off quantitatively and involve stakeholders in an explicit prioritization decision

text: Was erstellt ein Architekt primär in der ENTWURFSPHASE?
text_en: What does an architect primarily CREATE during the design phase?
explanation: During the design phase, architects primarily create architectural views (context view, building block view, runtime view, deployment view) and documented architectural decisions with their rationale. These artifacts communicate the design to different audiences at appropriate levels of abstraction. Detailed class diagrams for every module, UAT scripts, project reports, and database schemas are produced by other roles or phases.

text: Klassifizieren Sie jede Architekturperspektive als FUNKTIONAL, TECHNISCH oder KONZEPTUELL.
text_en: Classify each architectural perspective as FUNCTIONAL, TECHNICAL, or CONCEPTUAL.
explanation: These three perspectives help organize architectural concerns: functional (what the system must do — its capabilities and behaviors), technical (how the system is built — technologies, platforms, infrastructure), and conceptual (the underlying design philosophy and principles that unify the architecture). The conceptual perspective is often the hardest to articulate but gives the architecture its coherence and conceptual integrity.

text: Was beschreibt am BESTEN das Verhältnis eines Architekten zu STAKEHOLDERN?
text_en: Which BEST describes an architect's relationship to STAKEHOLDERS?
explanation: Architects serve as the technical communication bridge — translating business and user needs into architectural decisions, and explaining technical constraints back to business stakeholders in terms they understand. This bidirectional communication role is essential: without it, architecture becomes disconnected from business needs, and business stakeholders make uninformed demands. The architect is not a gatekeeper, sponsor, or sole decision-maker.

text: Welche Sicht im 4+1-Sichtenmodell erfasst die LOGISCHE Struktur — die Zerlegung in Objekte oder Teilsysteme?
text_en: In the 4+1 view model, which view captures the LOGICAL structure — the decomposition into objects or subsystems?
explanation: In Kruchten's 4+1 view model, the Logical View captures the functional decomposition into objects, classes, or subsystems, addressing the end-user's functional concerns. The Process View addresses concurrency and runtime behavior. The Development View describes the static organization of the software for developers. The Physical View shows the mapping onto hardware. Scenarios (the '+1') illustrate the other four views through use cases.

text: Welches der folgenden ist eine typische RANDBEDINGUNG, die die Softwarearchitektur beeinflusst?
text_en: Which of the following is a typical CONSTRAINT that influences software architecture?
explanation: Constraints are external, non-negotiable conditions that limit architectural choices — they differ from requirements in that they cannot be traded off. 'Must run on existing on-premise Linux servers — no cloud allowed' is a hard technical constraint that eliminates entire categories of architectural solutions. User interface preferences, team process preferences, and vague performance wishes are not architectural constraints.

text: Was ist die Verantwortlichkeit des Architekten gegenüber TECHNISCHEN RISIKEN?
text_en: What is the architect's responsibility toward TECHNICAL RISK?
explanation: Identifying, assessing, and mitigating technical risks is a core architect responsibility. Architects must surface risks early — before they become expensive problems — and propose concrete mitigations such as proof-of-concept prototypes, architectural spikes, or alternative design options. Waiting until risks materialize, delegating entirely to the project manager, or eliminating all risk before any implementation begins are all inappropriate approaches.

choices.text_en: Transfer responsibility for all technical risks to the project manager

choices.text: Alle identifizierten Risiken eliminieren, bevor der erste Sprint beginnen darf

text: Welche DREI sind typische Wissensbereiche, die ein Softwarearchitekt besitzen sollte?
text_en: Which THREE are typical knowledge areas a software architect should possess?
explanation: Architects need three knowledge areas working together: technical knowledge (patterns, platforms, and architectural styles to make informed design choices), domain knowledge (understanding the business context so architectural decisions serve real needs), and methodological knowledge (documentation techniques, evaluation methods, and processes such as arc42, ATAM). Marketing or contract law are not core architect competencies.

text: Klassifizieren Sie jeden Wissensbereich als TECHNISCHES, DOMÄNEN- oder METHODISCHES Wissen für einen Architekten.
text_en: Classify each knowledge area as TECHNICAL, DOMAIN, or METHODOLOGICAL knowledge for an architect.
explanation: Architects combine three knowledge types: technical (how systems are built — platforms, patterns, integration styles), domain (what the system is for — the business rules, regulations, and processes in the subject area), and methodological (how to approach architecture work — documentation standards, evaluation methods). All three are necessary; a deficit in any one leads to architectures that are technically clever but wrong for the context, or contextually appropriate but poorly structured.

choices.text: Kenntnisse über Microservices, Messaging-Systeme und Cloud-Plattformen
choices.text_en: Knowledge of microservices, messaging systems, and cloud platforms

choices.text: Verständnis von Bankenregulierungen und Finanzprozessen
choices.text_en: Understanding of banking regulations and financial processes

choices.text: Kenntnisse des arc42-Templates und des Architecture-Decision-Record-Formats
choices.text_en: Knowledge of arc42 template and Architecture Decision Record formats

choices.text: Kenntnisse von ATAM und szenariobasierter Architekturbewertung
choices.text_en: Knowledge of ATAM and scenario-based architecture evaluation

text: Ein Architekt hat vor zwei Jahren eine wichtige Architekturentscheidung aufgrund einer bestimmten technischen Randbedingung getroffen. Diese Randbedingung existiert nicht mehr. Was sollte der Architekt tun?
text_en: An architect made a key architectural decision two years ago based on a specific technical constraint. That constraint no longer exists. What should the architect do?
explanation: Architectural decisions should be revisited when the context that motivated them changes significantly. The original constraint was the justification for the decision; with that constraint removed, the decision may no longer be optimal and a better alternative may now be available. Blindly preserving decisions regardless of context leads to unnecessary technical debt. However, the reassessment must be deliberate and transparent — not a silent change.

choices.text: Die Entscheidung angesichts des veränderten Kontexts überprüfen und neu bewerten
choices.text_en: Revisit and reassess the decision given the changed context

choices.text: Die Entscheidung unverändert lassen — Architekturänderungen bringen immer übermäßige Risiken mit sich
choices.text_en: Keep the decision unchanged — modifying architecture always introduces excessive risk

choices.text: Die Neubewertung an den Projektmanager delegieren
choices.text_en: Delegate the reassessment to the project manager

choices.text: Bis zum nächsten Major-Release warten, bevor eine Überprüfung stattfindet
choices.text_en: Wait until the next major version release before reconsidering

choices.text: Die Entscheidung stillschweigend aktualisieren, ohne das übrige Team zu informieren
choices.text_en: Update the decision silently without informing the rest of the team

text: Welche ZWEI sind Anzeichen dafür, dass eine Softwarearchitektur VERSAGT?
text_en: Which TWO are signs that a software architecture is FAILING?
explanation: A failing architecture shows characteristic symptoms: new features consistently take disproportionately long to implement (structural rigidity — the architecture resists change) and quality problems such as performance degradation and security incidents are accumulating (the architecture no longer achieves its quality goals). Using a well-known pattern or having documented architecture are not failure indicators; they are positive practices.

choices.text: Neue Features benötigen konsistent unverhältnismäßig lange zur Implementierung
choices.text_en: New features consistently take disproportionately long to implement

choices.text: Anhäufende Qualitätsprobleme wie Performance-Verschlechterung und Sicherheitsvorfälle
choices.text_en: Accumulating quality problems such as performance degradation and security incidents

choices.text: Das Team verwendet ein bewährtes, weitverbreitetes Architekturmuster
choices.text_en: The team uses a well-established, widely-adopted architectural pattern

choices.text: Das System besteht aus mehr als drei unabhängig deployten Services
choices.text_en: The system is composed of more than three independently deployed services

choices.text: Die Architektur ist mit dem arc42-Template dokumentiert
choices.text_en: The architecture is documented using the arc42 template

text: Warum ist KOMMUNIKATION eine Kernkompetenz eines Softwarearchitekten?
text_en: Why is COMMUNICATION a core skill for a software architect?
explanation: Architecture is inherently a social activity. Architects must explain complex technical decisions to non-technical stakeholders (in terms of business impact), persuade developers to follow architectural guidelines (through understanding, not mandate), and negotiate trade-offs between competing business and technical concerns — all requiring effective communication across diverse audiences. Communication is not a substitute for technical knowledge; both are necessary.

choices.text: Architektur muss von unterschiedlichen Zielgruppen mit verschiedenem Hintergrund verstanden und umgesetzt werden
choices.text_en: Architecture must be understood and acted upon by diverse audiences with different backgrounds

choices.text: Kommunikationsfähigkeiten können tiefes technisches Wissen ersetzen
choices.text_en: Communication skills can substitute for deep technical knowledge

choices.text: Architekten sind für das Verfassen aller projektweiten Kommunikationen und E-Mails verantwortlich
choices.text_en: Architects are responsible for writing all project-wide communications and emails

choices.text: Kommunikation ist nur erforderlich, wenn Architekturentscheidungen aktiv angefochten werden
choices.text_en: Communication is only needed when architectural decisions are actively disputed

choices.text: Schriftliche Dokumentation ist ausreichend — verbale und visuelle Kommunikation sind zweitrangig
choices.text_en: Written documentation is sufficient — verbal and visual communication are secondary

text: Was ist die Rolle des Architekten in der AGILEN Entwicklung?
text_en: What is the architect's role in AGILE development?
explanation: In agile development, architects participate continuously — making just-in-time decisions as the understanding of requirements deepens, collaborating closely with the development team, and evolving the architecture incrementally alongside the product. The 'ivory tower architect' who designs everything upfront and hands off a complete architecture document before Sprint 1 is an anti-pattern in agile contexts. Architecture and implementation co-evolve.

choices.text: Kontinuierlicher Teilnehmer, der Just-in-time-Entscheidungen trifft und die Architektur iterativ weiterentwickelt
choices.text_en: Continuous participant making just-in-time decisions and evolving architecture iteratively

choices.text: Vorab-Designer, der vor Sprint 1 eine vollständige Architektur erstellt
choices.text_en: Upfront designer who produces a complete architecture before Sprint 1 begins

choices.text: Ein externer Berater, der nur bei auftretenden Architekturproblemen hinzugezogen wird
choices.text_en: An external consultant brought in only when architectural problems arise

choices.text: Der Scrum Master, der Sprint-Zeremonien moderiert und Hindernisse beseitigt
choices.text_en: The scrum master who facilitates sprint ceremonies and removes impediments

choices.text: Ein Beobachter mit beratender Funktion und ohne Mitspracherecht bei der Sprint-Planung
choices.text_en: An observer with an advisory role and no vote in sprint planning

text: Ein Softwarearchitekt beginnt ein neues Projekt und muss verstehen, welche Arten von Anforderungen Architekturentscheidungen beeinflussen werden. Welche ZWEI Kategorien von Anforderungen haben den STÄRKSTEN direkten Einfluss auf die Architektur?
text_en: A software architect is starting a new project and needs to understand which types of requirements will drive architectural decisions. Which TWO categories of requirements have the MOST direct impact on architecture?
explanation: Functional requirements describe what the system must do (features, behaviors, data); quality requirements describe how well it must do it (performance, security, reliability). Both categories directly drive structural and deployment decisions. Constraints restrict choices but are not the same as requirements. Business requirements and process requirements are higher-level inputs that get translated into functional and quality requirements before they influence architecture.

choices.text: Funktionale Anforderungen
choices.text_en: Functional requirements

choices.text: Qualitätsanforderungen
choices.text_en: Quality requirements

choices.text: Geschäftsprozessanforderungen
choices.text_en: Business process requirements

choices.text: Berichts- und Analyseanforderungen
choices.text_en: Reporting and analytics requirements

choices.text: Integrationsanforderungen
choices.text_en: Integration requirements

text: Ein Product Owner erklärt: 'Während Verkaufsspitzenereignissen muss der Checkout-Service das Zehnfache unseres normalen Datenverkehrs verarbeiten können.' Welches ISO/IEC 25010-Qualitätsmerkmal adressiert diese Anforderung?
text_en: A product owner states: 'During peak sales events, the checkout service must handle ten times our normal traffic.' Which ISO/IEC 25010 quality characteristic does this requirement address?
explanation: Performance efficiency in ISO 25010 covers time behavior, resource utilization, and capacity — including the ability to handle increased concurrent load. Reliability covers continuity of service but not load capacity per se. Scalability is not a top-level ISO 25010 characteristic; it falls under the capacity sub-characteristic of Performance efficiency.

choices.text: Performance-Effizienz
choices.text_en: Performance efficiency

choices.text: Zuverlässigkeit
choices.text_en: Reliability

choices.text: Sicherheit
choices.text_en: Security

choices.text: Wartbarkeit
choices.text_en: Maintainability

choices.text: Übertragbarkeit
choices.text_en: Portability

text: Eine veraltete Bankanwendung erfordert häufige Aktualisierungen, um sich entwickelnden Vorschriften zu entsprechen. Das Team benötigt Wochen für jede Änderung, da keine klaren Komponentengrenzen definiert sind. Welches Qualitätsmerkmal ist hier das HAUPTPROBLEM?
text_en: A legacy banking application requires frequent updates to comply with evolving regulations. The team spends weeks on each change because no component boundaries are clearly defined. Which quality characteristic is the PRIMARY concern here?
explanation: Maintainability covers modularity, reusability, analysability, modifiability, and testability — all aspects of how easy the system is to change. The described pain (costly changes, unclear boundaries) is a maintainability deficiency. Reliability concerns continued operation under failure. Compatibility concerns co-existence with other systems. While portability and usability are quality attributes, they do not relate to internal changeability.

choices.text: Wartbarkeit
choices.text_en: Maintainability

choices.text: Zuverlässigkeit
choices.text_en: Reliability

choices.text: Benutzerfreundlichkeit
choices.text_en: Usability

choices.text: Kompatibilität
choices.text_en: Compatibility

choices.text: Sicherheit
choices.text_en: Security

text: Eine Qualitätsanforderung lautet: 'Der Zahlungsservice muss 99,9 % der Zeit verfügbar sein, gemessen monatlich.' Welches ISO 25010-Qualitätsmerkmal wird hierdurch adressiert, und welches Teilmerkmal passt am besten?
text_en: A quality requirement states: 'The payment service must be available 99.9% of the time, measured monthly.' Which ISO 25010 quality characteristic does this address, and which sub-characteristic is the closest match?
explanation: Availability is a sub-characteristic of Reliability in ISO 25010. It measures the probability that the system is operational and accessible when needed. Performance efficiency relates to speed and capacity, not uptime. Security covers data protection. Portability covers environment transfer. Fault tolerance is a related sub-characteristic but refers to continued operation during faults, whereas availability is the broader uptime measure.

choices.text: Zuverlässigkeit (Teilmerkmal Verfügbarkeit)
choices.text_en: Reliability (availability sub-characteristic)

choices.text: Performance-Effizienz
choices.text_en: Performance efficiency

choices.text: Sicherheit
choices.text_en: Security

choices.text: Übertragbarkeit
choices.text_en: Portability

choices.text: Zuverlässigkeit (Teilmerkmal Fehlertoleranz)
choices.text_en: Reliability (fault tolerance sub-characteristic)

text: Im Anforderungsdokument eines Projekts steht: 'Das System soll schnell auf Benutzeraktionen reagieren.' Während der Architekturebewertung führt diese Anforderung zu Unstimmigkeiten – einige Teammitglieder interpretieren sie als 100 ms, andere als 2 Sekunden. Was ist die Grundursache dieses Problems?
text_en: A project's requirement document states: 'The system shall respond quickly to user actions.' During architecture evaluation, this requirement causes disagreement — some team members interpret it as 100ms, others as 2 seconds. What is the root cause of this problem?
explanation: Quality requirements become testable and unambiguous only when expressed with specific metrics, thresholds, and conditions: for example, 'Response time < 200ms at the 95th percentile under 1,000 concurrent users.' Vague terms like 'quickly' cannot be verified or used to guide design decisions. ISO 25010 classification alone does not resolve ambiguity. Stakeholder approval does not make a vague requirement measurable. Assigning the requirement to a sprint does not define acceptance criteria.

choices.text: Der Anforderung fehlen spezifische Metriken, Schwellenwerte und Bedingungen – sie kann nicht gemessen oder verifiziert werden
choices.text_en: The requirement lacks specific metrics, thresholds, and conditions — it cannot be measured or verified

choices.text: Die Anforderung wurde in natürlicher Sprache statt in einer formalen Spezifikationssprache verfasst
choices.text_en: The requirement was written in natural language instead of a formal specification language

choices.text: Die Anforderung wurde nicht der richtigen ISO 25010-Kategorie zugeordnet
choices.text_en: The requirement was not classified under the correct ISO 25010 category

choices.text: Die Anforderung wurde nicht von allen Stakeholdern vor der Architekturphase geprüft
choices.text_en: The requirement was not reviewed by all stakeholders before the architecture phase

choices.text: Die Anforderung wurde keinem bestimmten Entwicklungs-Sprint zugewiesen
choices.text_en: The requirement was not assigned to a specific development sprint

text: Ein Architekt erhebt Anforderungen für eine neue Gesundheitsplattform. Welche ZWEI Stakeholder-Gruppen liefern am wahrscheinlichsten Anforderungen, die Architekturentscheidungen direkt einschränken?
text_en: An architect is eliciting requirements for a new healthcare platform. Which TWO stakeholder groups are MOST likely to provide requirements that directly constrain architectural decisions?
explanation: End users define functional needs and usability requirements that shape the interaction model and data structures. Operations teams define availability, monitoring, deployment, and infrastructure requirements that constrain deployment architecture. Both groups produce requirements with direct structural implications. The architect themselves makes decisions but is not a requirements source. Marketing teams provide product positioning input but not technical architectural requirements.

choices.text: Endbenutzer (Benutzerfreundlichkeit, funktionale Features)
choices.text_en: End users (usability, functional features)

choices.text: Operations-Team (Verfügbarkeit, Monitoring, Deployment)
choices.text_en: Operations team (availability, monitoring, deployment)

choices.text: Der Architekt selbst
choices.text_en: The architect themselves

choices.text: Der Projektmanager (Zeitplan und Budget)
choices.text_en: The project manager (schedule and budget)

choices.text: Das Marketingteam (Branding-Richtlinien)
choices.text_en: The marketing team (branding guidelines)

text: Während einer Architekturüberprüfung für ein Zahlungsgateway muss das Team die Anforderung 'Das System muss zuverlässig sein' so konkretisieren, dass Architekturoptionen dagegen bewertet werden können. Welche Technik ist hierfür am BESTEN geeignet?
text_en: During an architecture review for a payment gateway, the team needs to make the requirement 'the system must be reliable' concrete enough to evaluate architectural options against it. Which technique is BEST suited for this?
explanation: A quality scenario describes a specific stimulus (event), its source, the system's context, the required response, and a measurable response measure. For example: 'When the primary database fails (stimulus), during normal operation (context), the system switches to the replica within 30 seconds with no data loss (response measure).' This makes the abstract goal 'reliable' concrete and testable. A user story describes functional features. A test script comes after design. An SLA is an output, not a design tool. Component diagrams show structure but do not express quality goals.

choices.text: Ein Qualitätsszenario: eine konkrete Beschreibung eines Stimulus, Kontexts und messbaren Antwortverhaltens
choices.text_en: A quality scenario: a concrete description of a stimulus, context, and measurable response

choices.text: Eine User Story, die den Fehlerfall im agilen Format beschreibt
choices.text_en: A user story describing the failure in agile format

choices.text: Ein Testskript, das das QA-Team während des Integrationstests ausführt
choices.text_en: A test script for the QA team to execute during integration testing

choices.text: Ein Komponentendiagramm, das zeigt, welche Services den Failover übernehmen
choices.text_en: A component diagram showing which services handle failover

choices.text: Ein SLA-Dokument, das mit dem Kunden vereinbart wurde
choices.text_en: An SLA document agreed with the client

text: Ein Architekt für ein Finanzinstitut entwirft eine neue Handelsplattform. Welche DREI der folgenden Punkte sind RANDBEDINGUNGEN, die die Entwurfsmöglichkeiten des Architekten einschränken – im Gegensatz zu Qualitätsanforderungen?
text_en: An architect for a financial institution is designing a new trading platform. Which THREE of the following are CONSTRAINTS that limit the architect's design choices — as opposed to quality requirements?
explanation: Constraints are non-negotiable given conditions that eliminate entire categories of solutions. An approved technology stack (organizational standard), regulatory compliance mandates (legal), and an unreplaceable legacy clearing system (existing infrastructure) are all constraints — they must be satisfied regardless of their impact on other qualities. Response time and availability targets are quality requirements: they restrict the solution space but leave the architect free to choose how to achieve them through design.

choices.text: Organisationsstandard: Es dürfen nur zugelassene Technologien aus dem Enterprise-Stack verwendet werden
choices.text_en: Organizational standard: only approved technologies from the enterprise stack may be used

choices.text: Regulatorische Anforderung: Alle Handelsdaten müssen innerhalb der EU gespeichert werden (MiFID II)
choices.text_en: Regulatory requirement: all trade data must be stored within the EU (MiFID II)

choices.text: Bestehende Infrastruktur: Das Legacy-Clearingsystem kann 3 Jahre lang nicht ersetzt werden
choices.text_en: Existing infrastructure: the legacy clearing system cannot be replaced for 3 years

choices.text: Die Orderausführung muss im 99. Perzentil innerhalb von 10 ms abgeschlossen sein
choices.text_en: Order execution must complete within 10ms at the 99th percentile

choices.text: Die Plattform muss während der Handelszeiten eine Verfügbarkeit von 99,99 % aufrechterhalten
choices.text_en: The platform must maintain 99.99% availability during trading hours

choices.text: Alle sensiblen Daten müssen im Ruhezustand und bei der Übertragung verschlüsselt sein
choices.text_en: All sensitive data must be encrypted at rest and in transit

text: Ein Cloud-Migrationsprojekt muss sowohl eine hohe Datenverarbeitungsdurchsatzrate als auch eine Ende-zu-Ende-Verschlüsselung aller Kundendatensätze gewährleisten. Das Team stellt fest, dass diese Ziele die Architektur in entgegengesetzte Richtungen ziehen. Welche ZWEI Qualitätsmerkmale stehen hier in Konflikt?
text_en: A cloud migration project must satisfy both high-throughput data processing and end-to-end encryption of all customer records. The team discovers these goals pull the architecture in opposite directions. Which TWO quality characteristics are in tension here?
explanation: Performance efficiency and security frequently conflict: encryption, input validation, and audit logging all consume CPU cycles and add latency, reducing throughput. Availability and maintainability can also conflict (redundancy complicates updates), but in this scenario the explicit tension is between processing speed and data protection. Usability and portability are not in tension with each other in any meaningful structural sense. Functional suitability sub-characteristics (completeness, correctness) do not conflict with each other.

choices.text: Performance-Effizienz (Verschlüsselung und Validierung verringern den Durchsatz)
choices.text_en: Performance efficiency (encryption and validation reduce throughput)

choices.text: Sicherheit (Verschlüsselung aller Kundendatensätze erforderlich)
choices.text_en: Security (encryption of all customer records required)

choices.text: Benutzerfreundlichkeit und Übertragbarkeit
choices.text_en: Usability and portability

choices.text: Kompatibilität und Zuverlässigkeit
choices.text_en: Compatibility and reliability

choices.text: Funktionale Vollständigkeit und funktionale Korrektheit
choices.text_en: Functional completeness and functional correctness

text: Der Product Owner stellt in einer Sprint-Planning-Sitzung fünf Anforderungen vor. Ordnen Sie jede dem richtigen ISO 25010-Qualitätsmerkmal zu.
text_en: The product owner presents five requirements during a sprint planning session. Classify each under the correct ISO 25010 quality characteristic.
explanation: ISO 25010 provides a standard taxonomy for quality characteristics used in requirements elicitation and architecture evaluation. Response time maps to Performance efficiency (time behavior). Automatic recovery maps to Reliability (recoverability). Authentication requirements map to Security. Developer onboarding time maps to Maintainability (analysability). Time-to-add-a-feature maps to Maintainability (modifiability). Correct classification ensures each requirement drives appropriate architectural decisions.

choices.text: Antwortzeit < 300 ms im 95. Perzentil
choices.text_en: Response time < 300ms at 95th percentile

choices.text: Das System stellt sich automatisch innerhalb von 30 s nach einem Fehler wieder her
choices.text_en: System recovers automatically within 30s after failure

choices.text: Alle API-Endpunkte erfordern Authentifizierungstoken
choices.text_en: All API endpoints require authentication tokens

choices.text: Neue Entwickler werden innerhalb von 2 Wochen produktiv
choices.text_en: New developers become productive within 2 weeks

choices.text: Das Hinzufügen einer neuen Zahlungsmethode dauert weniger als 5 Tage
choices.text_en: Adding a new payment method takes less than 5 days

text: Ein Projekt-Stakeholder besteht darauf: 'Wir müssen PostgreSQL verwenden – es ist die einzige von unserem Enterprise Architecture Board genehmigte Datenbank.' Ein anderer Stakeholder ergänzt: 'Und Suchabfragen müssen Ergebnisse in unter 500 ms liefern.' Wie sollte ein Architekt diese beiden Eingaben klassifizieren?
text_en: A project stakeholder insists: 'We must use PostgreSQL — it is the only database approved by our enterprise architecture board.' Another stakeholder adds: 'And search queries must return results in under 500ms.' How should an architect classify these two inputs?
explanation: The PostgreSQL mandate is a constraint — a non-negotiable given condition that eliminates design alternatives regardless of technical merit. The 500ms target is a quality requirement — a desired property that the architect must achieve through design choices (indexing strategy, caching, query optimization), but it leaves freedom in how to achieve it. Constraints are not simply 'harder' quality requirements; they belong to a different category entirely and are handled differently in architecture work.

choices.text: PostgreSQL-Mandat = Randbedingung; 500-ms-Ziel = Qualitätsanforderung
choices.text_en: PostgreSQL mandate = constraint; 500ms target = quality requirement

choices.text: Beides sind Randbedingungen, da beide den Entwurfsraum einschränken
choices.text_en: Both are constraints because both restrict the design space

choices.text: Beides sind Qualitätsanforderungen, da beide die Architektur beeinflussen
choices.text_en: Both are quality requirements because both influence the architecture

choices.text: PostgreSQL-Mandat = Qualitätsanforderung; 500-ms-Ziel = Randbedingung
choices.text_en: PostgreSQL mandate = quality requirement; 500ms target = constraint

choices.text: PostgreSQL-Mandat = funktionale Anforderung; 500-ms-Ziel = Randbedingung
choices.text_en: PostgreSQL mandate = functional requirement; 500ms target = constraint

text: Während einer Anforderungsprüfung sagt ein Stakeholder: 'Das System soll schnell sein.' Der Architekt stellt Nachfragen, aber der Stakeholder kann keine weiteren Details nennen. Warum ist diese Anforderung für die Architekturarbeit NICHT verwendbar, und was muss der Architekt tun?
text_en: During a requirements review, a stakeholder says: 'The system should be fast.' The architect asks follow-up questions but the stakeholder cannot elaborate further. Why is this requirement NOT usable in architecture work, and what must the architect do?
explanation: 'Fast' is unmeasurable without a metric (e.g., response time), a threshold (e.g., < 200ms), and a context (e.g., 95th percentile under 500 concurrent users). Without these, the requirement cannot guide design decisions or be verified after implementation. The architect must work with stakeholders to add specificity. Using a formal notation alone does not add measurability. The word 'should' in RFC 2119 terms indicates a recommendation, but that is not the root problem here — the real issue is missing quantification.

choices.text: Es fehlen Metrik, Schwellenwert und Kontext – der Architekt muss spezifische, messbare Kriterien erheben
choices.text_en: It has no metric, threshold, or context — the architect must elicit specific, measurable criteria

choices.text: Geschwindigkeit ist ein nachrangiges Anliegen; der Architekt sollte es niedrig priorisieren
choices.text_en: Speed is a secondary concern; the architect should deprioritize it

choices.text: Das Wort 'soll' macht die Anforderung optional und damit für die Architektur irrelevant
choices.text_en: The word 'should' makes it optional and therefore irrelevant to architecture

choices.text: Der Architekt sollte sie in ISO 25010-Notation umformulieren, um sie nutzbar zu machen
choices.text_en: The architect should rewrite it in ISO 25010 notation to make it usable

choices.text: Es handelt sich um eine Randbedingung, keine Qualitätsanforderung, und sollte entsprechend behandelt werden
choices.text_en: It is a constraint, not a quality requirement, and should be treated differently

text: Eine Online-Einzelhandelsplattform erlebte während eines Flash-Sales einen 4-stündigen Ausfall, als ein einzelner Anwendungsserver ausfiel. Der Architekt muss das System neu entwerfen, um dies zu verhindern. Welche ZWEI Architekturmaßnahmen verbessern die VERFÜGBARKEIT direkt?
text_en: An online retail platform experienced 4 hours of downtime during a flash sale when a single application server failed. The architect must redesign the system to prevent this. Which TWO architectural tactics DIRECTLY improve AVAILABILITY?
explanation: Redundancy (running multiple instances so a single failure does not cause an outage) and automatic failover (detecting failure and routing traffic to healthy instances without manual intervention) are the primary availability tactics. Unit tests improve reliability of logic but do not keep the system running during infrastructure failure. Encrypting data at rest improves security, not availability. A single powerful server is a single point of failure — it makes the problem worse, not better.

choices.text: Redundanz – mehrere Instanzen, damit ein einzelner Ausfall keinen Totalausfall verursacht
choices.text_en: Redundancy — multiple instances so one failure does not cause a full outage

choices.text: Automatischer Failover auf Backup-Instanzen, wenn eine primäre Instanz ausfällt
choices.text_en: Automatic failover to backup instances when a primary fails

choices.text: Erhöhung der Unit-Test-Abdeckung in der gesamten Anwendung
choices.text_en: Increasing unit test coverage across the application

choices.text: Verschlüsselung aller Daten im Ruhezustand und bei der Übertragung
choices.text_en: Encrypting all data at rest and in transit

choices.text: Ersetzen mehrerer Server durch einen einzelnen, leistungsfähigeren Server
choices.text_en: Replacing multiple servers with a single, more powerful server

text: Eine Gesundheitsdatenplattform speichert Patientendaten und stellt diese über REST-APIs bereit. Ein Sicherheitsaudit stellt fest, dass Daten unverschlüsselt übertragen werden und API-Endpunkte keine Zugangskontrolle haben. Welche ZWEI Architekturmaßnahmen adressieren diese Befunde am direktesten?
text_en: A healthcare data platform stores patient records and exposes them via REST APIs. A security audit identifies that data is transmitted unencrypted and API endpoints have no access control. Which TWO architectural tactics most directly address these findings?
explanation: Authentication and authorization at every API entry point address the access control finding. Encrypting sensitive data in transit (TLS) and at rest addresses the unencrypted transmission finding. Together these cover the two identified attack surfaces. Deployment frequency is a DevOps concern unrelated to security. A layered architecture is a structural style that may support security but is not itself a security tactic. Adding logging statements improves observability but does not prevent unauthorized access or data exposure.

choices.text: Authentifizierung und Autorisierung an jedem API-Eintrittspunkt
choices.text_en: Authentication and authorization enforced at every API entry point

choices.text: Verschlüsselung sensibler Daten bei der Übertragung (TLS) und im Ruhezustand
choices.text_en: Encrypting sensitive data in transit (TLS) and at rest

choices.text: Erhöhung der Deployment-Frequenz, um Sicherheits-Patches schneller auszurollen
choices.text_en: Increasing deployment frequency to roll out security patches faster

choices.text: Einführung einer Schichtenarchitektur mit einem dedizierten API-Gateway-Layer
choices.text_en: Adopting a layered architecture with a dedicated API gateway layer

choices.text: Hinzufügen von strukturiertem Logging an allen API-Endpunkten
choices.text_en: Adding structured logging to all API endpoints

text: Eine Microservices-Plattform ist auf 40 Services angewachsen. Das Team berichtet, dass jede Änderung am Order-Service ein Verständnis seiner Interaktionen mit dem Inventory-, Notification- und Payment-Service erfordert. Fehlerbehebungen beeinträchtigen regelmäßig nicht verwandte Services. Welche ZWEI Architekturmaßnahmen würden die Wartbarkeit am meisten verbessern?
text_en: A microservices platform has grown to 40 services. The team reports that every change to the order service requires understanding how it interacts with the inventory, notification, and payment services. Bug fixes routinely break unrelated services. Which TWO architectural tactics would MOST improve maintainability?
explanation: Modularization with clear component boundaries (well-defined interfaces, limited responsibilities) enables isolated changes. Encapsulation hiding internal implementation details prevents unintended coupling — other services depend only on contracts, not internals. Together they reduce the blast radius of changes. Deploying all components on a single server is a deployment concern that does not address logical coupling. A shared database increases coupling by letting services depend on each other's schemas. Global variables are an implementation anti-pattern that maximizes coupling.

choices.text: Modularisierung mit klaren, stabilen Komponentengrenzen und expliziten Schnittstellen
