Fault tolerance is a separate quality concern not guaranteed by layering.

choices.text: Klare Trennung der Verantwortlichkeiten nach Abstraktionsebene
choices.text_en: Clear separation of concerns by abstraction level

choices.text: Jede Schicht kann mit begrenzten Auswirkungen auf andere ausgetauscht werden
choices.text_en: Each layer can be replaced with limited impact on others

choices.text: Beseitigt die Notwendigkeit von Schnittstellen zwischen Komponenten
choices.text_en: Eliminates the need for interfaces between components

choices.text: Garantiert die geringstmögliche Latenz
choices.text_en: Guarantees the lowest possible latency

choices.text: Bietet automatisch Fehlertoleranz
choices.text_en: Automatically provides fault tolerance

text: Welche ZWEI sind NACHTEILE einer strikten SCHICHTEN-Architektur?
text_en: Which TWO are DISADVANTAGES of a strict LAYERED architecture?
explanation: Strict layering requires every call to pass through all intermediate layers even when only the top and bottom layers need to communicate, adding latency overhead. Cross-cutting concerns such as logging and security can be difficult to implement cleanly because they span all layers, tending to accumulate as tangled code in lower layers. Layers can be independently tested using test doubles for adjacent layers. Layering is orthogonal to OOP and cloud deployment.

choices.text: Jeder Aufruf muss alle Zwischenschichten durchlaufen (Latenz-Overhead)
choices.text_en: Every call must pass through all intermediate layers (latency overhead)

choices.text: Querschnittliche Belange können sich in unteren Schichten ansammeln
choices.text_en: Cross-cutting concerns can accumulate in lower layers

choices.text: Schichten können nicht unabhängig getestet werden
choices.text_en: Layers cannot be independently tested

choices.text: Strikte Schichtung zwingt alle Komponenten, eine gemeinsame Datenbank zu nutzen
choices.text_en: Strict layering forces all components to share a single database

choices.text: Schichten-Architekturen können nicht in der Cloud deployt werden
choices.text_en: Layered architectures cannot be deployed in the cloud

text: Ein Zahlungsdienst stellt eine Methode `charge(amount, currency)` bereit, schaltet intern aber je nach Verfügbarkeit zwischen drei verschiedenen Zahlungsanbietern um. Aufrufer haben keine Kenntnis darüber, welcher Anbieter aktiv ist. Welches Entwurfsprinzip wird hier angewendet?
text_en: A payment service exposes a method `charge(amount, currency)` but internally switches between three different payment processors depending on availability. Callers have no knowledge of which processor is active. Which design principle is being applied?
explanation: Information hiding means each building block exposes only a defined interface; its internal implementation is invisible to callers. Here, callers depend only on the `charge` interface — the internal processor selection is hidden. This is distinct from data encryption (a security control), access modifiers (a language mechanism that supports but does not constitute information hiding), and documentation (which describes internals rather than hiding them).

choices.text: Informationsverbergung — jeder Baustein stellt nur eine definierte Schnittstelle bereit; Interna sind verborgen
choices.text_en: Information hiding — each building block exposes only a defined interface; internals are hidden

choices.text: Trennung der Verantwortlichkeiten — die Anbieterauswahllogik ist ein separates Anliegen
choices.text_en: Separation of concerns — the processor selection logic is a separate concern

choices.text: Abhängigkeitsinversion — der Aufrufer hängt von einer Abstraktion ab
choices.text_en: Dependency inversion — the caller depends on an abstraction

choices.text: Open/Closed-Prinzip — der Dienst ist für neue Anbieter offen, ohne Schnittstellenänderung
choices.text_en: Open/Closed Principle — the service is open for new processors without interface change

choices.text: Einzelverantwortlichkeit — der Dienst hat nur eine Aufgabe: Zahlungsverarbeitung
choices.text_en: Single Responsibility — the service handles only payment processing

text: Eine monolithische E-Commerce-Anwendung verarbeitet Benutzerauthentifizierung, Auftragsverarbeitung, Bestandsaktualisierungen und E-Mail-Benachrichtigungen innerhalb desselben Moduls. Entwickler berichten, dass jede Änderung an der Authentifizierungslogik die Auftragsverarbeitung gefährdet. Welches Architekturprinzip wird verletzt?
text_en: A monolithic e-commerce application handles user authentication, order processing, inventory updates, and email notifications all within the same module. Developers report that any change to authentication logic risks breaking order processing. Which architectural principle is being violated?
explanation: Separation of Concerns (SoC) means organizing the system so that each building block addresses one specific concern independently. When unrelated concerns (authentication, orders, inventory, notifications) are mixed in one module, changes to one concern ripple into others. Information hiding is also partially violated, but the root cause is that distinct concerns have not been separated. DRY is about eliminating code duplication — not directly relevant here. SRP is the class-level counterpart to SoC.

choices.text: Trennung der Verantwortlichkeiten — jeder Baustein sollte ein spezifisches Anliegen unabhängig adressieren
choices.text_en: Separation of Concerns — each building block should address one specific concern independently

choices.text: Don't Repeat Yourself — Logik ist im Modul dupliziert
choices.text_en: Don't Repeat Yourself — logic is duplicated across the module

choices.text: Open/Closed-Prinzip — das Modul ist nicht offen für Erweiterungen
choices.text_en: Open/Closed Principle — the module is not open for extension

choices.text: Abhängigkeitsinversion — das Modul hängt von konkreten Implementierungen ab
choices.text_en: Dependency Inversion — the module depends on concrete implementations

choices.text: Liskov-Substitution — Unterklassen erfüllen den Elternklassen-Vertrag nicht
choices.text_en: Liskov Substitution — subclasses do not preserve the parent contract

text: Was ist der Unterschied zwischen AFFERENTER und EFFERENTER Kopplung?
text_en: What is the difference between AFFERENT and EFFERENT coupling?
explanation: Afferent coupling (Ca) counts how many components depend ON this component (fan-in — it is widely used). Efferent coupling (Ce) counts how many components this component depends on (fan-out — it has many dependencies). High afferent coupling means the component is central and stable; changing its interface ripples widely. High efferent coupling means the component is fragile — it is impacted by changes in many others. They are not runtime vs. compile-time distinctions, nor database vs. UI distinctions.

choices.text: Afferent = eingehende Abhängigkeiten (Fan-in); Efferent = ausgehende Abhängigkeiten (Fan-out)
choices.text_en: Afferent = incoming dependencies (fan-in); Efferent = outgoing dependencies (fan-out)

choices.text: Afferent = Laufzeit-Kopplung; Efferent = Übersetzungszeit-Kopplung
choices.text_en: Afferent = runtime coupling; Efferent = compile-time coupling

choices.text: Afferent = Kopplung an externe Systeme; Efferent = Kopplung an interne Module
choices.text_en: Afferent = coupling to external systems; Efferent = coupling to internal modules

choices.text: Sie sind Synonyme für dieselbe Kopplungseigenschaft
choices.text_en: They are synonyms for the same coupling property

choices.text: Afferent = statische Kopplung; Efferent = dynamische Kopplung
choices.text_en: Afferent = static coupling; Efferent = dynamic coupling

text: Welches Entwurfsziel sollten Architekten hinsichtlich der Kopplung zwischen Bausteinen verfolgen?
text_en: Which design goal should architects pursue regarding coupling between building blocks?
explanation: Loose coupling minimizes dependencies while still achieving required functionality, making components easier to change and test independently. Zero coupling is impossible in a working system — components must collaborate somehow. Maximum coupling creates a brittle system where everything must change together. Eliminating runtime dependencies in favour of static coupling is not a general goal; many valid architectures rely on runtime binding. Distinguishing only between afferent and efferent types does not address the goal.

choices.text: Lose Kopplung — Abhängigkeiten minimieren und dabei die erforderliche Funktionalität erhalten
choices.text_en: Loose coupling — minimize dependencies while achieving required functionality

choices.text: Maximale Kopplung — alle Komponenten koordinieren sich direkt für beste Performance
choices.text_en: Maximum coupling — all components coordinate directly for best performance

choices.text: Null-Kopplung — keine Komponente hängt von einer anderen ab
choices.text_en: Zero coupling — no component depends on any other

choices.text: Nur statische Kopplung — alle Laufzeitabhängigkeiten eliminieren
choices.text_en: Static coupling only — eliminate all runtime dependencies

choices.text: Nur afferente Kopplung — ausgehende Abhängigkeiten sind akzeptabel
choices.text_en: Afferent coupling only — outgoing dependencies are acceptable

text: Ein Healthcare-Modul verwaltet Patientenregistrierung, Terminplanung und Abrechnung. Das Team hat Schwierigkeiten, nur die Abrechnungslogik in einem anderen System wiederzuverwenden. Welche Qualitätseigenschaft fehlt?
text_en: A healthcare module handles patient registration, appointment scheduling, and billing. The team finds it hard to reuse just the billing logic in another system. Which quality is lacking?
explanation: High cohesion means the elements within a building block are closely related and serve the same purpose. A module mixing patient registration, scheduling, and billing has low cohesion — unrelated responsibilities are grouped together, making it impossible to reuse one without dragging in the others. High coupling between components is a separate but related problem. A single public method is a code-level implementation choice, not an architectural quality. Sharing a programming language is irrelevant to reusability.

choices.text: Hohe Kohäsion — Elemente innerhalb eines Bausteins sollten eng verwandt sein und demselben Zweck dienen
choices.text_en: High cohesion — elements within a building block should be closely related and serve the same purpose

choices.text: Lose Kopplung — das Modul hängt von zu vielen externen Komponenten ab
choices.text_en: Loose coupling — the module depends on too many external components

choices.text: Informationsverbergung — das Modul legt zu viele interne Details offen
choices.text_en: Information hiding — the module exposes too many internal details

choices.text: Trennung der Verantwortlichkeiten — querschnittliche Belange werden nicht adressiert
choices.text_en: Separation of concerns — cross-cutting concerns are not addressed

choices.text: Open/Closed-Prinzip — das Modul kann nicht ohne Modifikation erweitert werden
choices.text_en: Open/Closed Principle — the module cannot be extended without modification

text: Das Open/Closed-Prinzip besagt, dass ein Modul offen für Erweiterungen, aber geschlossen für Modifikationen sein sollte. Welche Architektur-Technik erreicht dies typischerweise?
text_en: The Open/Closed Principle states a module should be open for extension but closed for modification. Which architectural technique typically achieves this?
explanation: OCP is achieved through abstractions (interfaces or abstract classes) that allow new implementations to be added without modifying existing code. The caller depends on the abstraction, not the concrete implementation, so adding a new implementation does not require changes to callers. Making classes final prevents both modification and extension. Version control branching manages code history but does not enforce OCP. Rewriting for each requirement is the opposite of OCP. Read-only access is a runtime constraint, not an architectural principle.

choices.text: Abstraktionen und Schnittstellen, die neue Implementierungen ohne Änderung bestehenden Codes ermöglichen
choices.text_en: Abstractions and interfaces allowing new implementations without changing existing code

choices.text: Alle Klassen als final markieren, um unbefugte Modifikationen zu verhindern
choices.text_en: Making all classes final to prevent unauthorized modification

choices.text: Versionskontroll-Branching für jede Erweiterung
choices.text_en: Version control branching for each extension

choices.text: Verwendung des Template-Method-Patterns, um alle Schritte an Unterklassen zu delegieren
choices.text_en: Using the Template Method pattern to delegate all steps to subclasses

choices.text: Das Modul in der Produktion auf Lesezugriff sperren
choices.text_en: Locking the module to read-only access in production

text: Das Abhängigkeitsinversionsprinzip besagt, dass hochrangige Module NICHT von niedrigrangigen Modulen abhängen sollten. Wovon sollten beide stattdessen abhängen?
text_en: The Dependency Inversion Principle says high-level modules should NOT depend on low-level modules. What should both depend on instead?
explanation: DIP states both high-level (business logic) and low-level (infrastructure) modules should depend on abstractions (interfaces). This decouples business logic from infrastructure details such as specific databases or messaging systems. A dependency injection container is a mechanism that helps wire implementations to abstractions — it supports DIP but is not what both modules should depend on. A shared global configuration object creates its own coupling. The lowest layer is precisely what DIP says high-level modules should NOT depend on.

choices.text: Abstraktionen (Schnittstellen)
choices.text_en: Abstractions (interfaces)

choices.text: Konkrete Implementierungen des niedrigrangigen Moduls
choices.text_en: Concrete implementations of the low-level module

choices.text: Ein gemeinsames globales Konfigurationsobjekt
choices.text_en: A shared global configuration object

choices.text: Die unterste Architekturschicht
choices.text_en: The lowest architectural layer

choices.text: Ein Dependency-Injection-Container
choices.text_en: A dependency injection container

text: Welche ZWEI sind Merkmale eines GUTEN Schnittstellenentwurfs?
text_en: Which TWO are characteristics of a GOOD interface design?
explanation: Good interfaces guide callers toward correct usage and make incorrect usage hard (easy to use correctly, hard to misuse). They are also minimal — exposing only what callers need and nothing more, which reduces the surface area for breakage. Exposing internal state defeats information hiding. Accepting any input type and converting internally can mask errors that callers should handle. Making interfaces as large as possible in anticipation of future needs violates YAGNI and increases coupling surface.

choices.text: Leicht korrekt zu verwenden und schwer zu missbrauchen
choices.text_en: Easy to use correctly and hard to misuse

choices.text: Minimal — legt nur das offen, was Aufrufer benötigen
choices.text_en: Minimal — exposes only what callers need

choices.text: Legt alle internen Zustände zur Optimierung durch den Aufrufer offen
choices.text_en: Exposes all internal state for caller optimization

choices.text: Akzeptiert beliebige Eingabetypen und konvertiert diese intern
choices.text_en: Accepts any input type and converts internally

choices.text: So groß wie möglich, um erwartete zukünftige Anforderungen zu unterstützen
choices.text_en: As large as possible to support anticipated future needs

text: Welche VIER der folgenden sind Beispiele für QUERSCHNITTLICHE Belange?
text_en: Which FOUR of the following are examples of CROSS-CUTTING concerns?
explanation: Cross-cutting concerns affect multiple or all building blocks system-wide and require architectural-level design decisions applied consistently. Persistence strategy, logging and exception handling, authentication and authorization, and inter-component communication protocol are classic examples — each one touches every service or component. The color scheme of one specific screen and variable naming in a single module are local decisions that affect only one place and are not cross-cutting.

choices.text: Persistenzstrategie
choices.text_en: Persistence strategy

choices.text: Protokollierung und Ausnahmebehandlung
choices.text_en: Logging and exception handling

choices.text: Authentifizierung und Autorisierung
choices.text_en: Authentication and authorization

choices.text: Kommunikationsprotokoll zwischen Komponenten
choices.text_en: Inter-component communication protocol

choices.text: Farbschema eines bestimmten Bildschirms
choices.text_en: Color scheme of one specific screen

choices.text: Variablenbenennung in einem Modul
choices.text_en: Variable naming in one module

text: Was ist der wesentliche Unterschied zwischen TOP-DOWN- und BOTTOM-UP-Entwurfsansätzen?
text_en: What is the key difference between TOP-DOWN and BOTTOM-UP design approaches?
explanation: Top-down starts from the overall system structure and decomposes it into subsystems and components. Bottom-up starts from existing components (libraries, services, legacy systems) and composes them into larger structures. Both approaches are used in practice and can be applied in agile or waterfall contexts. They do not necessarily produce identical architectures. Neither approach inherently requires more stakeholder involvement than the other.

choices.text: Top-down zerlegt aus der Gesamtstruktur heraus; Bottom-up setzt aus bestehenden Komponenten zusammen
choices.text_en: Top-down decomposes from overall structure; bottom-up composes from existing components

choices.text: Top-down ist für Wasserfall; Bottom-up ist nur für agile Projekte geeignet
choices.text_en: Top-down is for waterfall; bottom-up is only suitable for agile projects

choices.text: Top-down beginnt beim Datenbankschema; Bottom-up beginnt bei der Benutzeroberfläche
choices.text_en: Top-down starts from the database schema; bottom-up starts from the UI

choices.text: Sie erzeugen immer identische Architekturen, wenn sie auf dasselbe System angewendet werden
choices.text_en: They always produce identical architectures when applied to the same system

choices.text: Bottom-up erfordert immer mehr Stakeholder-Beteiligung als Top-down
choices.text_en: Bottom-up always requires more stakeholder involvement than top-down

text: Was zeigt eine BLACK-BOX-Sicht auf einen Baustein?
text_en: What does a BLACK BOX view of a building block show?
explanation: A black box view shows only the external interface — what the component provides (offered interfaces) and requires (required interfaces) — without revealing internal structure. This is what other components and callers need to use the component. The internal class structure, runtime sequence diagrams, deployment configuration, and internal algorithms are all white box details — they describe what is inside the component, not its external contract.

choices.text: Nur die externe Schnittstelle — was er bereitstellt und benötigt
choices.text_en: External interface only — what it provides and requires

choices.text: Vollständige interne Klassenstruktur und Beziehungen
choices.text_en: Complete internal class structure and relationships

choices.text: Laufzeit-Sequenzdiagramme mit internem Nachrichtenfluss
choices.text_en: Runtime sequence diagrams showing internal message flow

choices.text: Deployment-Konfiguration auf Hardware
choices.text_en: Deployment configuration on hardware

choices.text: Interne Algorithmen und Datenstrukturen
choices.text_en: Internal algorithms and data structures

text: Was zeigt eine WHITE-BOX-Sicht auf einen Baustein?
text_en: What does a WHITE BOX view of a building block reveal?
explanation: A white box view opens the component and shows its internal decomposition — sub-components, their interfaces, and the relationships between them. It is used when architects need to understand or document the internal structure of a component. The external interface is the black box view. Runtime performance, test coverage, and deployment infrastructure belong to other views (runtime, deployment).

choices.text: Interne Zerlegung: Teilbausteine, Schnittstellen und deren Beziehungen
choices.text_en: Internal decomposition: sub-components, interfaces, and their relationships

choices.text: Nur die externe Schnittstelle, die anderen Komponenten bereitgestellt wird
choices.text_en: Only the external interface provided to other components

choices.text: Laufzeit-Performance-Eigenschaften unter Last
choices.text_en: Runtime performance characteristics under load

choices.text: Testabdeckungsmetriken und Testergebnisse
choices.text_en: Test coverage metrics and test results

choices.text: Deployment-Infrastruktur und Hardware-Zuordnung
choices.text_en: Deployment infrastructure and hardware mapping

text: Eine Komponente X hängt von 15 anderen Komponenten ab, und nur 2 andere Komponenten hängen von X ab. Welches Problem deutet dies an?
text_en: A component X depends on 15 other components and only 2 others depend on X. Which problem does this indicate?
explanation: High efferent coupling (Ce=15) means X depends on too many other components. Any change to any of those 15 may break or require changes in X. This is a design smell indicating X has too many responsibilities or is poorly decomposed. High afferent coupling (Ca) would be the opposite — many components depending on X. Low cohesion is a separate internal quality. A cyclic dependency would require one of X's 15 dependencies to also depend on X, which is not stated.

choices.text: Hohe efferente Kopplung — X hat zu viele ausgehende Abhängigkeiten
choices.text_en: High efferent coupling — X has too many outgoing dependencies

choices.text: Hohe afferente Kopplung — zu viele Komponenten hängen von X ab
choices.text_en: High afferent coupling — too many components depend on X

choices.text: Geringe Kohäsion — X ist intern schlecht strukturiert
choices.text_en: Low cohesion — X is poorly organized internally

choices.text: Dies ist ideal — hohe ausgehende Kopplung zeigt gute Integration
choices.text_en: This is ideal — high outgoing coupling demonstrates good integration

choices.text: Zyklische Abhängigkeit zwischen X und anderen
choices.text_en: Cyclic dependency between X and others

text: Ein Modul hat hohe AFFERENTE Kopplung (viele Module hängen davon ab). Welche Architekturimplikation hat dies?
text_en: A module has high AFFERENT coupling (many modules depend on it). What architectural implication does this have?
explanation: High afferent coupling means the module is widely depended upon — it is a stable, central element of the architecture. Because many components rely on it, its interface must be carefully managed: any breaking change propagates to all dependents. It is not inherently a sign of over-engineering or poor design; shared utilities and core domain objects often have high afferent coupling legitimately. High afferent coupling does not indicate instability — in fact, Martin's instability metric shows high Ca correlates with low instability.

choices.text: Weit verbreitetes, stabiles Element — Schnittstellenänderungen müssen sorgfältig gehandhabt werden
choices.text_en: Widely-used stable element — interface changes must be managed carefully

choices.text: Sollte gelöscht werden — viele Abhängige zeigen immer Über-Engineering an
choices.text_en: Should be deleted — high dependents always indicate over-engineering

choices.text: Schlecht entworfen und sollte immer sofort aufgeteilt werden
choices.text_en: Poorly designed and should always be split immediately

choices.text: Instabil — anfällig für häufige Schnittstellenänderungen
choices.text_en: Unstable — prone to frequent interface changes

choices.text: Weist auf eine Sicherheitsschwachstelle durch breite Exposition hin
choices.text_en: Indicates a security vulnerability due to wide exposure

text: Was ist der Zweck von RÜCKKOPPLUNGSSCHLEIFEN während des Architekturentwurfs?
text_en: What is the purpose of FEEDBACK LOOPS during architecture design?
explanation: Short feedback loops — prototypes, spikes, conformance checks, architecture reviews — reveal incorrect assumptions and technical risks early, while the cost of correction is still low. This is a core risk-management technique in the iSAQB curriculum. Feedback loops do not replace architecture documentation nor generate new requirements. Waiting for stakeholder sign-off on every sprint is a governance process, not an architectural feedback mechanism. The goal is early correction, not final approval.

choices.text: Falsche Annahmen und Risiken früh erkennen, solange Korrekturen noch günstig sind
choices.text_en: Discover incorrect assumptions and risks early while correction is cheap

choices.text: Die endgültige Architektur genehmigen, bevor Code geschrieben wird
choices.text_en: Approve the final architecture before any code is written

choices.text: Detailliertere Anforderungen von Entwicklern generieren
choices.text_en: Generate more detailed requirements from developers

choices.text: Architekturdokumentation durch Echtzeit-Entwickler-Feedback ersetzen
choices.text_en: Replace architecture documentation with real-time developer feedback

choices.text: Sicherstellen, dass Stakeholder jeden Sprint formal genehmigen
choices.text_en: Ensure stakeholders formally sign off on every sprint

text: Wie erfüllt ein Architekt QUALITÄTSANFORDERUNGEN durch die Architektur?
text_en: How does an architect fulfill QUALITY REQUIREMENTS through architecture?
explanation: Quality requirements (performance, maintainability, availability, security) drive architectural design decisions. The architect selects patterns, structures, and technologies specifically because they support the required quality attributes. Delegating quality to QA after architecture is finalized means structural causes of quality problems cannot be corrected. Maximizing performance first may conflict with other quality attributes. Writing unit tests and requesting hardware budget address symptoms rather than architectural causes.

choices.text: Wählt Muster, Strukturen und Technologien aus, die die erforderlichen Qualitätsmerkmale unterstützen
choices.text_en: Selects patterns, structures, and technologies that support the required quality attributes

choices.text: Delegiert alle Qualitätsbelange an QA, nachdem die Architektur abgeschlossen ist
choices.text_en: Delegates all quality concerns to QA after architecture is finalized

choices.text: Maximiert zuerst die Performance und adressiert dann alle anderen Qualitätsmerkmale der Reihe nach
choices.text_en: Maximizes performance first, then addresses all other quality attributes in order

choices.text: Schreibt umfassende Unit-Tests für jede Komponente
choices.text_en: Writes comprehensive unit tests for every component

choices.text: Beantragt zusätzliches Hardware-Budget zur Lösung von Qualitätsmängeln
choices.text_en: Requests additional hardware budget to resolve quality shortfalls

text: Ordne jedes System dem passenden Architekturmuster zu: Schichten (Layers) oder Pipes und Filter.
text_en: Match each system to the architectural pattern that fits best: Layers or Pipes and Filters.
explanation: Layers suits systems decomposed by abstraction level with clear horizontal separation (UI / business logic / data access). Each layer depends only on the one below it. Pipes and Filters suits systems where data flows sequentially through independent processing stages, each stage transforming and passing data to the next without shared state.

choices.text: Web-Anwendung mit Präsentations-, Geschäftslogik- und Datenzugriffsschicht
choices.text_en: Web application with presentation, business logic, and data access layers

choices.text: Compiler: Lexer → Parser → Optimierer → Code-Generator
choices.text_en: Compiler: lexer → parser → optimizer → code generator

choices.text: E-Commerce-Backend mit UI-, Domänen- und Datenbankschicht
choices.text_en: E-commerce backend with UI, domain, and database layers

choices.text: Datenverarbeitungspipeline: Validierung → Transformation → Anreicherung → Ausgabe
choices.text_en: Data processing pipeline: validation → transformation → enrichment → output

text: Welche ZWEI sind Vorteile des PIPES-AND-FILTERS-Musters?
text_en: Which TWO are advantages of the PIPES AND FILTERS pattern?
explanation: Filters are independent processing units with no shared state, making them reusable and composable in different sequences. Because filters are independent, they can also process data in parallel when the pipeline allows it. Filters do not share state — they communicate only through the data stream. Each filter typically requires input data in a specific format, so format conversion between filters is often necessary. Filters may be implemented in different languages as long as they agree on the data format.

choices.text: Filter sind wiederverwendbar und in verschiedenen Reihenfolgen kombinierbar
choices.text_en: Filters are reusable and composable in different sequences

choices.text: Unabhängige Filter können Daten parallel verarbeiten
choices.text_en: Independent filters can process data in parallel

choices.text: Alle Filter teilen Zustand über einen zentralen Datenspeicher zur Koordination
choices.text_en: All filters share state through a central data store for coordination

choices.text: Das Muster beseitigt die Notwendigkeit der Dateiformat-Konvertierung zwischen Stufen
choices.text_en: The pattern eliminates the need for data format conversion between stages

choices.text: Erfordert, dass alle Filter dieselbe Programmiersprache verwenden
choices.text_en: Requires all filters to use the same programming language

text: Welche Domäne ist am BESTEN für das PIPES-AND-FILTERS-Muster geeignet?
text_en: Which domain is BEST suited to the PIPES AND FILTERS pattern?
explanation: Pipes and Filters excels at ETL pipelines and stream processing where data flows sequentially through independent transformation steps. Classic applications include compilers, media transcoding, log processing, and data enrichment pipelines. Interactive UIs require bidirectional event-driven interaction that Pipes and Filters does not model well. Transactional systems with shared database state benefit from layering. Real-time shared-state systems need low-latency in-memory communication, not pipeline stages.

choices.text: ETL-Pipelines und Stream-Verarbeitung mit sequentiellen Transformationen
choices.text_en: ETL pipelines and stream processing with sequential transformations

choices.text: Interaktive Benutzeroberflächen mit komplexer bidirektionaler Benutzerinteraktion
choices.text_en: Interactive UIs with complex bidirectional user interaction

choices.text: Transaktionale Bankensysteme mit stark konsistentem gemeinsamem Zustand
choices.text_en: Transactional banking systems with strongly consistent shared state

choices.text: Echtzeit-kollaboratives Bearbeiten mit geringer Latenz und gemeinsamem Zustand
choices.text_en: Real-time collaborative editing with low-latency shared state

choices.text: Dokumentenmanagementsysteme mit komplexen Suchanforderungen
choices.text_en: Document management systems with complex search requirements

text: Sie sind Architekt für eine große E-Commerce-Plattform. Teams verantworten Checkout, Bestand und Empfehlungen unabhängig voneinander. Unabhängiges Deployment und Team-Autonomie sind entscheidend. Welches Architekturmuster passt am BESTEN?
text_en: You are an architect for a large e-commerce platform. Teams own checkout, inventory, and recommendations independently. Independent deployability and team autonomy are critical. Which architectural pattern fits BEST?
explanation: Microservices are designed for independent deployment by autonomous teams, each owning a specific business capability with its own data store and deployment pipeline. This directly addresses the scenario's team structure and autonomy requirements. A layered architecture still tends toward a shared deployment unit. Pipes and Filters addresses sequential data processing, not team-owned business capabilities. A monolith requires coordinated deployment of all teams. Blackboard suits AI or expert-system scenarios with multiple knowledge sources.

choices.text: Microservices
choices.text_en: Microservices

choices.text: Schichten
choices.text_en: Layers

choices.text: Pipes und Filter
choices.text_en: Pipes and Filters

choices.text: Monolith
choices.text_en: Monolith

choices.text: Blackboard
choices.text_en: Blackboard

text: Was ist der wesentliche Unterschied zwischen STRIKTER und ENTSPANNTER Schichtung?
text_en: What is the key difference between STRICT and RELAXED layering?
explanation: In strict layering, each layer may only use the layer immediately below it — no skip-layer calls allowed. This enforces strong encapsulation but can add unnecessary indirection. In relaxed layering, a layer may call any lower layer directly, trading encapsulation for convenience and potentially better performance. Relaxed layering allows upward calls only in specific patterns like callbacks, not as a general rule — so the option 'relaxed allows upward calls' is a common misconception.

choices.text: Strikte Schichtung verbietet Schicht-überspringende Aufrufe; entspannte Schichtung erlaubt direkten Aufruf jeder unteren Schicht
choices.text_en: Strict prohibits skip-layer calls; relaxed allows any lower layer to be called directly

choices.text: Strikte Schichtung erfordert eine Programmiersprache; entspannte Schichtung erlaubt gemischte Sprachen
choices.text_en: Strict requires one programming language; relaxed allows mixed languages

choices.text: Entspannte Schichtung hebt alle Richtungskommunikationsbeschränkungen zwischen Schichten auf
choices.text_en: Relaxed removes all directional communication constraints between layers

choices.text: Strikte Schichtung schreibt vor, dass die UI immer die oberste Schicht ist
choices.text_en: Strict mandates the UI is always the topmost layer

choices.text: Entspannte Schichtung erlaubt unteren Schichten, obere Schichten frei aufzurufen
choices.text_en: Relaxed allows lower layers to freely call upper layers

text: Was ist der Zweck eines API GATEWAYS in einer Microservices-Architektur?
text_en: In a Microservices architecture, what is the purpose of an API GATEWAY?
explanation: An API Gateway is the single entry point for external clients. It handles cross-cutting concerns such as authentication, rate limiting, routing, and SSL termination — hiding the internal microservice topology from clients. It is not a message broker or database. It does not enforce a technology choice on services. Replacing service-specific databases with a shared store contradicts the microservices principle of data ownership. API documentation generation is a separate tooling concern.

choices.text: Einziger Einstiegspunkt, der Authentifizierung, Routing und Rate-Limiting für Clients übernimmt
choices.text_en: Single entry point handling authentication, routing, and rate limiting for clients

choices.text: Gemeinsamer Message-Broker für die gesamte asynchrone Kommunikation zwischen Diensten
choices.text_en: Shared message broker for all asynchronous inter-service communication

choices.text: Zwingt alle Microservices, dieselbe Programmiersprache zu verwenden
choices.text_en: Enforces all microservices to use the same programming language

choices.text: Ersetzt individuelle Dienstdatenbanken durch einen gemeinsamen Datenspeicher
choices.text_en: Replaces individual service databases with a single shared data store

choices.text: Generiert automatisch API-Dokumentation aus dem Dienst-Quellcode
choices.text_en: Automatically generates API documentation from service source code

text: Was ist KONZEPTIONELLE INTEGRITÄT in der Softwarearchitektur?
text_en: What is CONCEPTUAL INTEGRITY in software architecture?
explanation: Conceptual integrity means the architecture follows a coherent, consistent set of design ideas applied throughout. It makes the architecture predictable, understandable, and easier to extend correctly — new contributors can anticipate how things should fit together. It does not require a single author; it requires consistent principles. Using one language or avoiding libraries does not create conceptual integrity. Passing a formal review or centralizing all documentation are governance activities, not integrity properties.

choices.text: Ein kohärenter, konsistenter Satz von Entwurfsideen, der durchgehend im System angewendet wird
choices.text_en: Consistent set of design ideas applied coherently throughout the system

choices.text: Gesamter Quellcode von einer Person geschrieben, um Konsistenz zu gewährleisten
choices.text_en: All source code written by the same person to ensure consistency

choices.text: Einzelne Programmiersprache ohne externe Bibliotheken
choices.text_en: Single programming language with no external libraries

choices.text: Architektur, die ein formales Review-Gremium bestanden hat
choices.text_en: Architecture that has passed a formal review board

choices.text: Alle Entscheidungen in einem zentralen Architekturdokument dokumentiert
choices.text_en: All decisions documented in one central architecture document

text: Das Robustheitsprinzip (Postelsches Gesetz) besagt: 'Sei konservativ in dem, was du sendest, und tolerant in dem, was du akzeptierst.' Was ist seine architektonische Bedeutung?
text_en: The Robustness Principle (Postel's Law) states 'Be conservative in what you send, liberal in what you accept.' What is its architectural significance?
explanation: Postel's Law improves integration resilience: tolerant input handling prevents failures at integration points when upstream systems send slightly non-conforming data, while strict output ensures downstream systems are not burdened with unexpected edge cases. It applies broadly to API and service interface design, not only to network protocols. It is not purely about security; it is about integration robustness. It does not mandate JSON; it is format-agnostic.

choices.text: Tolerante Eingabeverarbeitung verhindert Integrationsfehler; strikte Ausgabe schützt nachgelagerte Systeme
choices.text_en: Tolerant input handling prevents integration failures; strict output protects downstream systems

choices.text: Alle Schnittstellen müssen konservative (minimale) Datenformate verwenden, um die Nutzlast zu reduzieren
choices.text_en: All interfaces must use conservative (minimal) data formats to reduce payload size

choices.text: Ein Sicherheitsprinzip ausschließlich für Eingabevalidierung und Ausgabe-Sanitisierung
choices.text_en: A security principle for input validation and output sanitization only

choices.text: Gilt nur für Netzwerkprotokolle, nicht für Anwendungsschnittstellen
choices.text_en: Applies only to network protocols, not application service interfaces

choices.text: Erfordert, dass alle APIs ausschließlich JSON akzeptieren und zurückgeben
choices.text_en: Requires all APIs to accept and return JSON exclusively

text: Ein Team erwägt Microservices für ein neues internes HR-Tool mit 20 Benutzern. Was ist der WICHTIGSTE Kompromiss, der berücksichtigt werden muss?
text_en: A team is considering Microservices for a new internal HR tool with 20 users. What is the MOST important trade-off to consider?
explanation: Microservices have significant operational overhead: service discovery, distributed tracing, network failure handling, independent deployments, and per-service monitoring. For a small system with 20 users, this overhead almost certainly outweighs the benefits. A modular monolith would deliver the same separation of concerns at much lower cost. GDPR does not prevent using microservices for HR data. There is no minimum user threshold for microservices — the criterion is organizational and scalability need. HR domains can be modelled with well-defined service boundaries.

choices.text: Der Betriebsaufwand überwiegt bei weitem die Vorteile für diese Größenordnung und Nutzerbasis
choices.text_en: Operational overhead far outweighs the benefits for this scale and user base

choices.text: Microservices können keine HR-Daten DSGVO-konform speichern
choices.text_en: Microservices cannot store HR data in compliance with GDPR

choices.text: Microservices erfordern mindestens 1.000 gleichzeitige Benutzer, um praktikabel zu sein
choices.text_en: Microservices require a minimum of 1,000 concurrent users to be viable

choices.text: Microservices sind für jedes neue Greenfield-Projekt immer vorzuziehen
choices.text_en: Microservices are always preferable for any new greenfield project

choices.text: Die HR-Domäne kann nicht mit klaren Servicegrenzen modelliert werden
choices.text_en: The HR domain cannot be modelled with clear service boundaries

text: Welche ZWEI sind VORTEILE von Microservices im Vergleich zu einem geschichteten Monolithen?
text_en: Which TWO are ADVANTAGES of Microservices compared to a layered monolith?
explanation: Microservices enable independent scalability (only the services under load need to scale) and technology diversity (each service can use the programming language, framework, or database best suited to its domain). These are genuine structural advantages. Microservices introduce more network latency and distributed complexity, not less. They typically require more developers and DevOps effort. Strong consistency across services is harder to achieve, not easier, due to the distributed nature of the system.

choices.text: Einzelne Dienste können unabhängig skaliert werden
choices.text_en: Individual services can be scaled independently

choices.text: Verschiedene Dienste können pro Domäne unterschiedliche Technologien verwenden
choices.text_en: Different services can use different technologies per domain

choices.text: Beseitigt Netzwerklatenz und alle Komplexität verteilter Systeme
choices.text_en: Eliminates network latency and all distributed system complexity

choices.text: Erfordert weniger Entwickler als ein gleichwertiger Monolith
choices.text_en: Requires fewer developers than an equivalent monolith

choices.text: Bietet automatisch starke Konsistenz über alle Dienste hinweg
choices.text_en: Automatically provides strong consistency across all services

text: Welche ZWEI Entwurfsprinzipien wirken ZUSAMMEN, um lose gekoppelte, kohäsive Bausteine zu schaffen?
text_en: Which TWO design principles work TOGETHER to create loosely coupled, cohesive building blocks?
explanation: Separation of Concerns (SoC) ensures each component handles one concern — promoting high cohesion within a component. Information Hiding ensures each component hides its internals — promoting low coupling between components. Applied together: high cohesion within, loose coupling between. Dependency injection is an implementation technique that can support DIP but does not itself create cohesion or reduce coupling as a principle. A single codebase increases coupling. Maximum coupling is the opposite of the goal.

choices.text: Trennung der Verantwortlichkeiten — jede Komponente übernimmt einen Belang
choices.text_en: Separation of Concerns — each component handles one concern

choices.text: Informationsverbergung — jede Komponente verbirgt ihre Interna
choices.text_en: Information Hiding — each component hides its internals

choices.text: Maximale Kopplung — alle Komponenten teilen gegenseitiges Wissen
choices.text_en: Maximum coupling — all components share knowledge of each other

choices.text: Einzelne Codebasis — alle Codes zusammen zu halten reduziert den Integrationsaufwand
choices.text_en: Single codebase — keeping all code together reduces integration effort

choices.text: Dependency Injection — Laufzeit-Zusammenstellung aller Komponenten
choices.text_en: Dependency injection — runtime assembly of all components

text: Ein Architekt beschreibt einer Zahlungskomponente gegenüber Stakeholdern: 'Eine Black Box, die eine Zahlungsanforderung akzeptiert und eine Bestätigung oder Ablehnung zurückgibt — Sie müssen nicht wissen, wie sie mit der Bank kommuniziert.' Welches Entwurfsprinzip veranschaulicht dies?
text_en: An architect describes a payment component to stakeholders as 'a black box that accepts a payment request and returns a confirmation or decline — you do not need to know how it talks to the bank.' Which design principle does this illustrate?
explanation: Abstraction means identifying essential characteristics (what the component does) while hiding unnecessary implementation detail (how it communicates with the bank). At the architectural level, abstraction allows stakeholders and other components to reason about a building block purely in terms of its interface. This is related to but distinct from information hiding (which is about protecting internals) and SoC (which is about assigning one concern per component). Abstract Factory is a GoF pattern, not the general principle of abstraction.

choices.text: Abstraktion — wesentliche Eigenschaften werden offengelegt, unnötige Implementierungsdetails verborgen
choices.text_en: Abstraction — essential characteristics are exposed while unnecessary implementation detail is hidden

choices.text: Trennung der Verantwortlichkeiten — Zahlung ist von der Bankkommunikation getrennt
choices.text_en: Separation of Concerns — payment is separated from banking communication

choices.text: Einzelverantwortlichkeit — die Komponente hat eine Aufgabe
choices.text_en: Single Responsibility Principle — the component has one job

choices.text: Open/Closed-Prinzip — die Komponente ist für neue Zahlungsarten offen
choices.text_en: Open/Closed Principle — the component is open for new payment types

choices.text: Anwendung des Abstract-Factory-Entwurfsmusters
choices.text_en: Applying the Abstract Factory design pattern

text: Sie entwerfen ein Datenverarbeitungssystem mit den Stufen: Validierung → Transformation → Anreicherung → Ausgabe, sequentiell angewendet. Welches Architekturmuster passt am BESTEN?
text_en: You are designing a data-processing system with stages: validation → transformation → enrichment → output, applied in sequence. Which architectural pattern fits BEST?
explanation: Pipes and Filters is designed exactly for sequential data-processing pipelines where each stage (filter) transforms data independently and passes it to the next via a pipe. Each stage is replaceable without affecting others. Layers separates abstraction levels, not sequential processing stages. Microservices would add distributed overhead for no benefit in a single pipeline. Blackboard is for problems solved by multiple cooperating knowledge sources, not sequential stages. Event-driven architecture suits asynchronous, reactive scenarios — not a strictly sequential pipeline.

choices.text: Pipes und Filter
choices.text_en: Pipes and Filters

choices.text: Schichten
choices.text_en: Layers

choices.text: Microservices
choices.text_en: Microservices

choices.text: Blackboard
choices.text_en: Blackboard

choices.text: Ereignisgesteuerte Architektur
choices.text_en: Event-driven architecture

text: Wann wird TECHNISCHE SCHULDEN zu einem ARCHITEKTURELLEN Risiko?
text_en: When does TECHNICAL DEBT become an ARCHITECTURAL risk?
explanation: Technical debt becomes an architectural risk when it accumulates in foundational structural components — the building blocks that many other parts depend on. Debt in these areas degrades maintainability systemically, slows all future changes, and increases the risk of regression across the entire system. A high bug count, developer overtime, or failing tests are operational signals that may correlate with debt but do not in themselves make debt an architectural risk. System age is irrelevant without examining the structural state.

choices.text: Wenn sie sich in grundlegenden Strukturkomponenten ansammelt und alle Änderungen verlangsamt
choices.text_en: When it accumulates in foundational structural components making all changes slower

choices.text: Wenn die Gesamtzahl der Fehler einen im Qualitätsplan definierten Schwellenwert überschreitet
choices.text_en: When total bug count exceeds a threshold defined in the quality plan

choices.text: Wenn Entwickler mehr als zwei aufeinanderfolgende Wochen Überstunden machen
choices.text_en: When developers work overtime for more than two consecutive weeks

choices.text: Wenn das System älter als fünf Jahre ist, unabhängig von der Codequalität
choices.text_en: When the system is older than five years regardless of code quality

choices.text: Wenn mehr als 10 % der automatisierten Tests in einem Release fehlschlagen
choices.text_en: When more than 10% of automated tests fail in a release

text: Was ist das STRANGLER-FIG-Muster als Migrationsstrategie?
text_en: What is the STRANGLER FIG pattern as a migration strategy?
explanation: The strangler fig pattern incrementally replaces parts of a legacy system. New functionality is built alongside the old system; a facade or proxy gradually routes traffic to new implementations until the legacy can be retired. This avoids the high risk of a 'big bang' rewrite. Adding a caching layer does not replace legacy components. Running old and new systems permanently in parallel is dual-maintenance, not strangler fig. A new API wrapper that never retires the legacy is the Anti-Corruption Layer pattern, not strangler fig.

choices.text: Schrittweises Ersetzen von Legacy-Komponenten, indem Traffic peu à peu auf neue Implementierungen umgeleitet wird
choices.text_en: Incrementally replacing legacy components by routing traffic to new implementations piece by piece

choices.text: Vollständiges Neuschreiben des Systems in einem koordinierten Big-Bang-Release
choices.text_en: Rewriting the entire system from scratch in one coordinated big-bang release

choices.text: Hinzufügen einer Caching-Schicht vor Legacy-Komponenten zur Performance-Verbesserung
choices.text_en: Adding a caching layer in front of legacy components to improve performance

choices.text: Dauerhaftes paralleles Betreiben von altem und neuem System als langfristige Strategie
choices.text_en: Running old and new systems permanently in parallel as a long-term strategy

choices.text: Das Legacy-System einfrieren und einen neuen API-Wrapper bereitstellen, ohne das Legacy zu stilllegen
choices.text_en: Freezing the legacy system and exposing a new API wrapper without retiring the legacy

text: Welche ZWEI beschreiben das OPEN/CLOSED-PRINZIP (OCP) korrekt?
text_en: Which TWO describe the OPEN/CLOSED PRINCIPLE (OCP) correctly?
explanation: OCP: a module is open for extension (new behavior can be added) and closed for modification (existing, tested code should not need to change when extending). This is achieved through abstraction and polymorphism. Marking all classes as final prevents both modification and extension. Restricting committer access is a team process, not an architectural principle. 'Open for reading, closed for writing' misapplies database read/write semantics to a software design principle.

choices.text: Offen für Erweiterungen — neues Verhalten kann hinzugefügt werden, ohne bestehenden Code zu ändern
choices.text_en: Open for extension — new behavior can be added without changing existing code

choices.text: Geschlossen für Modifikationen — bestehender Code ändert sich nicht bei der Erweiterung des Verhaltens
choices.text_en: Closed for modification — existing code does not change when extending behavior

choices.text: Alle Klassen müssen als final markiert werden, um unbefugte Modifikationen zu verhindern
choices.text_en: All classes must be marked final to prevent unauthorized modification

choices.text: Nur ein Entwickler darf gleichzeitig Änderungen an einer Komponente einreichen
choices.text_en: Only one developer may commit changes to a component at a time

choices.text: Offen zum Lesen, geschlossen zum Schreiben — wie eine schreibgeschützte Datenbankansicht
choices.text_en: Open for reading, closed for writing — like a read-only database view

text: Welche ZWEI sind Vorteile der Verwendung von SCHNITTSTELLEN zur Umsetzung des Abhängigkeitsinversionsprinzips?
text_en: Which TWO are benefits of using INTERFACES to implement the Dependency Inversion Principle?
explanation: By depending on interfaces rather than concrete classes: (1) callers are decoupled from specific implementations — the calling module does not know which concrete class fulfils the interface; (2) implementations can be swapped or replaced without modifying calling code, enabling easier testing with mock implementations and technology substitution. Interfaces do not eliminate the need for unit tests — they make testing easier by allowing injection of test doubles. Interfaces do not enforce performance requirements. Interfaces can help break cyclic dependencies but do not prevent them in all cases.

choices.text: Aufrufer werden von konkreten Implementierungen entkoppelt
choices.text_en: Callers are decoupled from concrete implementations

choices.text: Implementierungen können ausgetauscht werden, ohne den aufrufenden Code zu ändern
choices.text_en: Implementations can be swapped without modifying calling code

choices.text: Schnittstellen beseitigen die Notwendigkeit von Unit-Tests durch die Durchsetzung von Verträgen
choices.text_en: Interfaces eliminate the need for unit tests by enforcing contracts

choices.text: Schnittstellen erzwingen automatisch Performance-Anforderungen zur Übersetzungszeit
choices.text_en: Interfaces automatically enforce performance requirements at compile time

choices.text: Schnittstellen verhindern zyklische Abhängigkeiten in allen Architekturkonfigurationen
choices.text_en: Interfaces prevent cyclic dependencies in all architectural configurations

text: Eine verteilte Banking-Plattform muss eine einheitliche Caching-Strategie über ihre Konto-, Transaktions- und Benachrichtigungsdienste hinweg anwenden. Wie sollte der Architekt diesen QUERSCHNITTLICHEN BELANG handhaben?
text_en: A distributed banking platform must apply a consistent caching strategy across its account, transaction, and notification services. How should the architect handle this CROSS-CUTTING CONCERN?
explanation: Cross-cutting concerns affecting many components should be addressed architecturally — as a centrally specified concept that all components follow consistently. Ad-hoc, per-team caching implementations lead to inconsistency, duplication, and unexpected interactions. Disabling caching avoids the problem rather than solving it. Caching only at the UI layer does not help backend services. Assigning caching to one team siloes the decision away from the components that apply it.

choices.text: Eine zentral definierte Caching-Strategie festlegen, der alle Komponenten konsistent folgen
choices.text_en: Define a centrally-specified caching strategy all components follow consistently

choices.text: Jedem Service-Team erlauben, Caching unabhängig nach eigenen Präferenzen zu implementieren
choices.text_en: Allow each service team to implement caching independently per their preference

choices.text: Caching in allen Diensten deaktivieren, um die querschnittliche Komplexität zu vermeiden
choices.text_en: Disable caching across all services to avoid the cross-cutting complexity

choices.text: Caching nur in der UI-Schicht implementieren, um Backend-Dienste abzuschirmen
choices.text_en: Implement caching only in the UI layer to shield backend services

choices.text: Caching-Verantwortung ausschließlich dem Datenbankteam übertragen
choices.text_en: Assign caching responsibility exclusively to the database team

text: Welche ZWEI Kriterien sollten Entscheidungen über die KOMPONENTENGRANULARITÄT leiten?
text_en: Which TWO criteria should guide decisions about COMPONENT GRANULARITY?
explanation: Granularity should be guided by: a single clear responsibility per component (each component has one concern — too coarse means multiple concerns mixed; too fine means trivial components with excessive coordination overhead) and the need for independent deployment or replacement (if a part needs to change or scale independently, it merits its own component). Lines of code is a weak proxy — components doing very different things may be similar in size. Matching component count to team count confuses Conway's Law with architectural principles.

choices.text: Einzelne klare Verantwortlichkeit (Trennung der Belange)
choices.text_en: Single clear responsibility (separation of concerns)

choices.text: Bedarf an unabhängigem Deployment oder Austausch
choices.text_en: Need for independent deployment or replacement

choices.text: Alle Komponenten sollten dieselbe Größe in Codezeilen haben
choices.text_en: All components should be the same size in lines of code

choices.text: Gesamtzahl der Komponenten minimieren, unabhängig von der Klarheit der Verantwortlichkeiten
choices.text_en: Minimize total number of components regardless of responsibility clarity

choices.text: Komponentenanzahl muss der Anzahl der Entwicklungsteams entsprechen
choices.text_en: Component count must match the number of development teams

text: Welche ZWEI sind gültige RÜCKKOPPLUNGSSCHLEIFEN-Mechanismen, die ein Architekt etablieren sollte?
text_en: Which TWO are valid FEEDBACK LOOP mechanisms an architect should establish?
explanation: Short feedback loops detect problems early. Automated architecture conformance checks in CI/CD catch deviations from intended structure automatically with every build. Regular architecture reviews with the development team surface misunderstandings and design drift before they solidify. Waiting until system testing to evaluate decisions is too late — costly structural changes are unavoidable by that point. Diagrams generated only from production reflect the current state but provide no early warning. Annual reviews are too infrequent.

choices.text: Automatisierte Architektur-Konformitätsprüfungen in der CI/CD-Pipeline
choices.text_en: Automated architecture conformance checks in CI/CD pipeline

choices.text: Regelmäßige Architektur-Reviews mit dem Entwicklungsteam
choices.text_en: Regular architecture review sessions with the development team

choices.text: Warten bis zum Systemtest, um Architekturentscheidungen zu bewerten
choices.text_en: Waiting until system testing to evaluate architectural decisions

choices.text: Architekturdiagramme nur aus dem produktiven System generieren
choices.text_en: Generating architecture diagrams only from the deployed production system

choices.text: Jährliches Architektur-Review ausschließlich mit externen Beratern
choices.text_en: Annual architecture review with external consultants only

text: Klassifiziere jedes Entwurfsprinzip danach, ob es primär die Kopplung reduziert oder die Kohäsion erhöht.
text_en: Classify each design principle by whether it primarily reduces coupling or primarily increases cohesion.
explanation: Coupling relates to dependencies between building blocks; cohesion relates to the internal consistency of a building block's responsibilities. Information hiding reduces coupling by shielding internals from callers. Dependency Inversion reduces coupling by making high-level modules depend on abstractions rather than concrete implementations. Single Responsibility increases cohesion by ensuring a class has only one reason to change. Separation of Concerns increases cohesion by grouping related responsibilities in the same building block.

choices.text: Informationsverbergung (Information Hiding)
choices.text_en: Information hiding

choices.text: Einzelverantwortlichkeit (Single Responsibility)
choices.text_en: Single Responsibility

choices.text: Abhängigkeitsinversion (Dependency Inversion)
choices.text_en: Dependency Inversion

choices.text: Trennung der Verantwortlichkeiten (Separation of Concerns)
choices.text_en: Separation of Concerns

text: Wann sollte die 'BUILD vs. BUY'-Entscheidung als ARCHITEKTURENTSCHEIDUNG behandelt werden?
text_en: When should the 'BUILD vs. BUY' decision be treated as an ARCHITECTURE decision?
explanation: Build vs. buy becomes an architecture decision when it affects system structure, integration points, or constraints on future evolution. Purchasing a COTS product creates dependencies on the vendor's APIs, data models, upgrade cycles, and extensibility limits — all structural concerns. It is not purely a procurement decision. The threshold is structural impact, not cost, user count, or whether the system faces customers.

choices.text: Wenn die Wahl die Systemstruktur, Integrationspunkte oder zukünftige Weiterentwicklung beeinflusst
choices.text_en: When the choice affects system structure, integration points, or future evolution

choices.text: Nur wenn die Produktkosten einen bestimmten Budgetschwellenwert übersteigen
choices.text_en: Only when the product cost exceeds a specific budget threshold

choices.text: Build vs. Buy ist immer eine Beschaffungsentscheidung, niemals eine Architekturentscheidung
choices.text_en: Build vs. buy is always a procurement decision, never an architectural one

choices.text: Nur wenn mehr als 100 Endnutzer direkt betroffen sind
choices.text_en: Only when more than 100 end users are directly affected

choices.text: Nur für extern kundenorientierte Systeme, nicht für interne Tools
choices.text_en: Only for external customer-facing systems, not internal tools

text: Warum sollten Architekten Abhängigkeiten zwischen Bausteinen VERWALTEN und REDUZIEREN?
text_en: Why should architects MANAGE and REDUCE dependencies between building blocks?
explanation: Unnecessary dependencies increase coupling, making it harder to change, test, or replace components independently. Reducing dependencies improves maintainability and enables parallel development by different teams. Dependencies do not directly reduce lines of code. Some necessary dependencies cannot be eliminated — the goal is to minimize unnecessary ones. Runtime performance may or may not improve when dependencies are reduced. Team size is determined by domain complexity, not dependency count.

choices.text: Weniger Abhängigkeiten ermöglichen es, Komponenten unabhängig zu ändern und zu testen
choices.text_en: Fewer dependencies allow components to be changed and tested independently

choices.text: Weniger Abhängigkeiten reduzieren direkt die Gesamtzahl der Codezeilen
choices.text_en: Fewer dependencies directly reduce the total number of lines of code

choices.text: Alle Abhängigkeiten sind von Natur aus schädlich und müssen vollständig beseitigt werden
choices.text_en: All dependencies are inherently harmful and must be eliminated completely

choices.text: Die Reduzierung von Abhängigkeiten verbessert immer die Laufzeit-Performance
choices.text_en: Reducing dependencies always improves runtime performance

choices.text: Weniger Abhängigkeiten reduzieren die Anzahl der benötigten Entwickler im Projekt
choices.text_en: Fewer dependencies reduce the number of developers required on the project

text: Welche Aussage über das ABHÄNGIGKEITSINVERSIONSPRINZIP auf Architekturebene ist RICHTIG?
text_en: Which statement about the DEPENDENCY INVERSION PRINCIPLE at architectural level is TRUE?
explanation: DIP achieves loose coupling between business logic (high-level) and infrastructure (low-level) by having both depend on abstractions. Business logic defines interfaces; infrastructure implements them. This means the domain layer is insulated from changes to databases, messaging systems, or external APIs. High-level modules calling low-level modules directly is the problem DIP solves, not a recommendation. SRP (one reason to change) is a separate principle. LSP (subclass substitutability) is another SOLID principle. Independent deployability is a microservices concern, not a DIP requirement.

choices.text: Geschäftslogik hängt von Abstraktionen ab, nicht von konkreten Infrastrukturimplementierungen
choices.text_en: Business logic depends on abstractions, not concrete infrastructure implementations

choices.text: Hochrangige Module sollten niedrigrangige Module direkt aufrufen, für bessere Performance
choices.text_en: High-level modules should call low-level modules directly for better performance

choices.text: DIP stellt sicher, dass jede Klasse nur einen Grund zur Änderung hat
choices.text_en: DIP ensures every class has only one reason to change

choices.text: DIP verhindert, dass Unterklassen den Elternklassen-Vertrag brechen
choices.text_en: DIP prevents subclasses from breaking parent class contracts

choices.text: DIP erfordert, dass jedes Modul unabhängig deploybar ist
choices.text_en: DIP requires every module to be independently deployable

text: Welche ZWEI sind NACHTEILE von Microservices im Vergleich zu einem Monolithen?
text_en: Which TWO are DISADVANTAGES of Microservices compared to a monolith?
explanation: Microservices introduce distributed system complexity — network failures, partial failures, eventual consistency, and the need for distributed tracing. They also carry higher operational overhead: more deployment pipelines, independent monitoring stacks, service discovery, and container orchestration. These costs are real and must be justified by clear benefits such as team autonomy or independent scalability. Microservices work well in the cloud and explicitly support polyglot technology choices. Service count is not capped at any number.

choices.text: Komplexität verteilter Systeme: Netzwerkfehler und Teilausfälle
choices.text_en: Distributed system complexity: network failures and partial failures

choices.text: Höherer Betriebsaufwand: Monitoring, Deployments, Service Discovery
choices.text_en: Higher operational overhead: monitoring, deployments, service discovery

choices.text: Können nicht in Cloud-Umgebungen deployt werden
choices.text_en: Cannot be deployed in cloud environments

choices.text: Verhindert die Verwendung verschiedener Programmiersprachen für verschiedene Dienste
choices.text_en: Prevents the use of different programming languages across services

choices.text: Kann nicht mehr als 10 Dienste in einem System unterstützen
choices.text_en: Cannot support more than 10 services in a single system

text: Was ist die INSTABILITÄTS-Metrik (I) für ein Modul, und was zeigt hohe Instabilität an?
text_en: What is the INSTABILITY metric (I) for a module, and what does high instability indicate?
explanation: Instability I = Ce / (Ca + Ce), where Ce is efferent coupling and Ca is afferent coupling. A value near 1 (high instability) means the module has many outgoing dependencies and few incoming — it depends on many others but is not widely depended upon. Such modules are volatile: they are often changed in response to changes in their dependencies. The formula Ca/(Ca+Ce) would give the inverse (stability). Total raw dependencies without normalisation is a different metric. Instability applies to all modules, not only databases.

choices.text: I = Ce/(Ca+Ce); hohe Instabilität = viele ausgehende Abhängigkeiten, anfällig für Änderungen
choices.text_en: I = Ce/(Ca+Ce); high instability = many outgoing dependencies, prone to change

choices.text: I = Ca/(Ca+Ce); hohe Instabilität = von vielen anderen abhängig
choices.text_en: I = Ca/(Ca+Ce); high instability = widely depended upon by others

choices.text: I = Gesamtzahl der Abhängigkeiten; höher ist immer besser für die Integration
choices.text_en: I = total number of dependencies; higher is always better for integration

choices.text: Die Instabilitätsmetrik ist nur für Datenbankzugriffsmodule relevant
choices.text_en: The instability metric is only relevant for database access modules

choices.text: Hohe Instabilität zeigt an, dass das Modul unzureichende Testabdeckung hat
choices.text_en: High instability indicates the module has insufficient test coverage

text: Welche ZWEI sind gültige Ansätze zur Implementierung eines SCHICHTEN-Musters?
text_en: Which TWO are valid approaches for implementing a LAYERS pattern?
explanation: Layers can be implemented strictly (each layer calls only the immediately adjacent lower layer — stronger encapsulation, more indirection) or in relaxed form (a layer may call any lower layer directly — less encapsulation, potentially less overhead). Both are valid depending on requirements. Bidirectional layers would allow lower layers to call upper layers freely, creating circular dependencies — this breaks layering. Parallel layers defined purely by threading is not a layering concept. Circular layers create cyclic dependencies, which undermines the pattern entirely.

choices.text: Strikte Schichten — jede Schicht ruft nur die direkt darunter liegende Schicht auf
choices.text_en: Strict layers — each layer only calls the immediately lower layer

choices.text: Entspannte Schichten — eine Schicht darf jede beliebige untere Schicht direkt aufrufen
choices.text_en: Relaxed layers — a layer may call any lower layer directly

choices.text: Bidirektionale Schichten — untere Schichten dürfen obere Schichten frei aufrufen
choices.text_en: Bidirectional layers — lower layers may freely call upper layers

choices.text: Parallele Schichten — jede Schicht läuft ausschließlich in einem eigenen Thread
choices.text_en: Parallel layers — each layer runs in a separate thread exclusively

choices.text: Kreisförmige Schichten — die oberste Schicht ruft die unterste Schicht auf, um einen Zyklus zu bilden
choices.text_en: Circular layers — the topmost layer calls the bottommost layer to complete a cycle

text: Was ist der PRIMÄRE architektonische Vorteil des PIPES-AND-FILTERS-Musters gegenüber einer monolithischen Verarbeitungsschleife?
text_en: What is the PRIMARY architectural benefit of the PIPES AND FILTERS pattern over a monolithic processing loop?
explanation: Each filter in Pipes and Filters is an independent, reusable processing unit. New processing steps can be inserted and existing steps reordered or replaced without modifying others — achieving OCP at the architectural level. A monolithic loop must be modified every time a step changes. The pattern does not guarantee the lowest latency (inter-filter data passing can add overhead). Filters do not share a central data store — they communicate only through the data stream. Data format conversion between filters is often required. Filters do not auto-scale.

choices.text: Unabhängige, wiederverwendbare Filter ermöglichen das Hinzufügen von Schritten ohne Änderung bestehenden Codes
choices.text_en: Independent, reusable filters enable adding steps without modifying existing code

choices.text: Das Muster garantiert die geringstmögliche Verarbeitungslatenz
choices.text_en: The pattern guarantees the lowest possible processing latency

choices.text: Alle Filter teilen einen zentralen Datenspeicher für die Zustandskoordination
choices.text_en: All filters share a central data store for shared state coordination

choices.text: Das Muster beseitigt die Notwendigkeit der Dateiformat-Konvertierung zwischen Stufen
choices.text_en: The pattern eliminates the need for data format conversion between stages

choices.text: Filter skalieren automatisch auf jede Last ohne Konfiguration
choices.text_en: Filters automatically scale to any load without configuration

text: Ordne jede Aussage dem passenden Architekturmuster zu: Schichten, Pipes und Filter oder Microservices.
text_en: Match each statement to the architectural pattern it best describes: Layers, Pipes and Filters, or Microservices.
explanation: Layers separates abstraction levels horizontally — each layer depends only on the layer below it. Pipes and Filters decomposes a system into independent sequential data-processing stages that communicate only through the data stream. Microservices decomposes by autonomous business capability: each service owns its own data store, is independently deployable, and can use its own technology stack.

choices.text: Jeder Dienst kann unabhängig von anderen deployt werden
choices.text_en: Each service can be deployed independently of other services

choices.text: Daten durchlaufen eine Folge unabhängiger Transformationsschritte
choices.text_en: Data flows through a sequence of independent transformation steps

choices.text: Jede Schicht darf nur die direkt darunter liegende Schicht aufrufen (strenge Schichtung)
choices.text_en: Each layer may only call the layer directly below it (strict layering)

choices.text: Jeder Dienst besitzt seine eigene Datenhaltung
choices.text_en: Each service owns its own data store

choices.text: Filter kennen ihre Nachbarn nicht und kommunizieren nur über den Datenstrom
choices.text_en: Filters do not know their neighbours and communicate only via the data stream

text: Ein Teamleiter überprüft die Architekturdokumentation und stellt fest, dass sie das System so beschreibt, wie es vor 18 Monaten entworfen wurde – vor drei großen Refactoring-Sprints. Welche ZWEI Eigenschaften GUTER Architekturdokumentation werden dabei verletzt?
text_en: A team lead reviews the architecture documentation and notices it describes the system as it was designed 18 months ago, before three major refactoring sprints. Which TWO properties of GOOD architecture documentation are being violated?
explanation: Good documentation must be correct/current (reflects the actual architecture) and understandable by its target audience. Documentation that has diverged from the real system is worse than none — it actively misleads readers. Exhaustiveness is not a required property; stale comprehensive docs are still harmful.

choices.text_en: Correct and current

choices.text: Verständlich für die Zielgruppe
choices.text_en: Understandable by the target audience

choices.text: Vollständig – dokumentiert jede Klasse und Methode
choices.text_en: Exhaustive — documents every class and method
