choices.text_en: A performance benchmark suite executed against the production environment

choices.text: Ein UML-Einschränkungsdiagramm, das den Entwurf gegen dokumentierte Anforderungen validiert
choices.text_en: A UML constraint diagram that validates the design against documented requirements

text: Welche ZWEI sind Beispiele für FITNESS-FUNKTIONEN zur Architekturqualität?
text_en: Which TWO are examples of FITNESS FUNCTIONS for architecture quality?
explanation: Fitness functions enforce architectural rules automatically and objectively. A CI test that fails when cyclic package dependencies are introduced is an automated structural check. An automated latency check that fails when P99 response time exceeds 200ms is a behavioral fitness function. Both are objective, repeatable, and run without human involvement. Manual checklists and whiteboard sessions are qualitative methods, not fitness functions.

choices.text: CI-Test, der fehlschlägt, wenn eine zyklische Paketabhängigkeit eingeführt wird
choices.text_en: CI test that fails when any cyclic package dependency is introduced

choices.text: Automatisierte Latenzprüfung: 'API-P99-Antwortzeit muss unter 200 ms liegen'
choices.text_en: Automated latency check: 'API P99 response time must be under 200ms'

choices.text: Manuelle Code-Review-Checkliste, die vom leitenden Architekten geprüft und unterzeichnet wird
choices.text_en: Manual code review checklist reviewed and signed by the lead architect

choices.text: Whiteboard-Walkthrough des vorgeschlagenen Entwurfs mit dem Entwicklungsteam
choices.text_en: Whiteboard walkthrough of the proposed design with the development team

choices.text: Sprint-Retrospektive-Diskussion über Architekturqualitätsbedenken
choices.text_en: Sprint retrospective discussion of architecture quality concerns

text: Nach zwei Jahren Entwicklung zeigt ein Analysetool, dass UI-Komponenten einer Retail-Anwendung direkt Datenbankzugriffsklassen importieren, was der dokumentierten Schichtenarchitektur widerspricht. Um welche Art von Bewertung handelt es sich und was stellt der Befund dar?
text_en: After two years of development, an analysis tool reveals that UI components in a retail application are directly importing database access classes, contradicting the documented layered architecture. What type of evaluation is this, and what does the finding represent?
explanation: Conformance evaluation checks whether the actual implemented architecture matches the intended/documented architecture. Finding that UI components bypass the service layer to call the database directly is a conformance violation — the system has drifted from its documented design. This is architecture erosion caused by undisciplined incremental changes over time.

choices.text: Architektur-Konformitätsbewertung — die Implementierung ist vom beabsichtigten Entwurf abgedriftet
choices.text_en: Architectural conformance evaluation — the implementation has drifted from the intended design

choices.text: Quantitative Kopplungsanalyse — die Metriken zeigen, dass die Schichtenarchitektur korrekt funktioniert
choices.text_en: Quantitative coupling analysis — the metrics show the layered architecture is working correctly

choices.text: ISO-25010-Konformitätsprüfung — Überprüfung, ob das System internationale Qualitätsstandards erfüllt
choices.text_en: ISO 25010 compliance check — verifying the system meets international quality standards

choices.text: ATAM-Bewertung — Stakeholder identifizieren Trade-offs im aktuellen Entwurf
choices.text_en: ATAM evaluation — stakeholders are identifying trade-offs in the current design

choices.text: Performance-Bewertung — direkter Datenbankzugriff aus der UI kann den Durchsatz verbessern
choices.text_en: Performance evaluation — directly accessing the database from the UI may improve throughput

text: Was ist ARCHITEKTURSCHULD (technische Schulden auf Architekturebene)?
text_en: What is ARCHITECTURAL DEBT (technical debt at the architecture level)?
explanation: Architectural debt is the accumulated cost of suboptimal architectural decisions made in the past — shortcuts or compromises that made delivery faster but created structural problems (high coupling, poor modularity, violated principles). Unlike code-level technical debt, it affects the structural properties of the system and is harder and more expensive to repay.

choices.text: Angesammelte Kosten suboptimaler vergangener Architekturentscheidungen, die durch Refactoring zurückgezahlt werden müssen
choices.text_en: Accumulated cost of suboptimal past architectural decisions that must be repaid through refactoring

choices.text: Die Summe der noch nicht umgesetzten Architekturanforderungen im Backlog
choices.text_en: The sum of unimplemented architectural requirements still in the backlog

choices.text: Performance-Overhead, der durch Abstraktionsschichten und Indirektion entsteht
choices.text_en: Performance overhead introduced by abstraction layers and indirection

choices.text: Architekturentscheidungen, die aufgeschoben wurden und noch nicht von Stakeholdern genehmigt sind
choices.text_en: Architecture decisions that were deferred and not yet approved by stakeholders

choices.text: Die Lücke zwischen der dokumentierten Architektur und dem, was Entwickler tatsächlich gebaut haben
choices.text_en: The gap between the documented architecture and what developers actually built

text: Welche DREI sind typische QUALITATIVE Architekturbewertungsmethoden?
text_en: Which THREE are typical QUALITATIVE architecture evaluation methods?
explanation: Qualitative methods rely on human judgment rather than numeric measurements: expert reviews (architects share informed opinions), ATAM-style scenario walkthroughs (stakeholders walk through quality scenarios), and architecture walkthroughs (team members review views and decisions together). All three produce insights and recommendations rather than numerical scores.

choices.text: Expertenbewertung durch erfahrene Architekten
choices.text_en: Expert review by experienced architects

choices.text: Szenariobasierte Bewertung (ATAM-Stil)
choices.text_en: Scenario-based evaluation (ATAM-style)

choices.text: Architektur-Walkthrough mit Stakeholdern
choices.text_en: Architecture walkthrough with stakeholders

choices.text: Messen der zyklomatischen Komplexität pro Modul mittels statischer Analyse
choices.text_en: Measuring cyclomatic complexity per module using static analysis

choices.text: Berechnung von afferenten/efferenten Kopplungsmetriken aus dem Quellcode
choices.text_en: Computing afferent/efferent coupling metrics from source code

choices.text: Automatisierter Performance-Lasttest zur Messung von API-Antwortzeiten
choices.text_en: Automated performance load test measuring API response times

text: Welche ZWEI sind typische QUANTITATIVE Architekturbewertungsmethoden?
text_en: Which TWO are typical QUANTITATIVE architecture evaluation methods?
explanation: Quantitative methods produce numeric measurements that can be tracked over time: coupling metrics (Ca, Ce, instability scores) measure structural properties of the code; automated latency measurements produce concrete response time data. Expert reviews and ATAM walkthroughs are qualitative because they produce judgments and findings, not numbers.

choices.text: Kopplungsmetriken: afferent (Ca), efferent (Ce) und Instabilität (I) pro Modul
choices.text_en: Coupling metrics: afferent (Ca), efferent (Ce), and instability (I) per module

choices.text: Automatisierte Performance-Latenzmessungen unter definierter Last
choices.text_en: Automated performance latency measurements under defined load

choices.text: Expertenbewertung und Kritik von Architekturentscheidungen durch Senior-Architekten
choices.text_en: Expert review and critique of architecture decisions by senior architects

choices.text: ATAM-szenariobasierter Walkthrough mit Stakeholdern
choices.text_en: ATAM scenario-based walkthrough with stakeholders

choices.text: Stakeholder-Workshop zur Erhebung und Priorisierung von Qualitätsattributen
choices.text_en: Stakeholder workshop to elicit and prioritize quality attributes

text: Modul X hat Ca=1 und Ce=9. Was lässt sich über seine Stabilität und das architektonische Risiko schlussfolgern?
text_en: Module X has Ca=1 and Ce=9. What can you conclude about its stability and the architectural risk it poses?
explanation: I = Ce/(Ca+Ce) = 9/10 = 0.9 — very high instability. The module depends heavily on 9 others (Ce=9) while only one module depends on it (Ca=1). Any change in those 9 dependencies can force changes in Module X, making it fragile. This is the opposite of a stable utility module, which would have high Ca and low Ce.

choices.text: Modul X ist hochgradig instabil (I=0,9) — fragil, weil es von vielen anderen abhängt
choices.text_en: Module X is highly unstable (I=0.9) — fragile because it depends on many others

choices.text: Modul X ist hochgradig stabil (I=0,1) — robust, weil es weit verbreitet genutzt wird
choices.text_en: Module X is highly stable (I=0.1) — robust because it is widely used by others

choices.text: Modul X ist wahrscheinlich an einer zyklischen Abhängigkeitskette beteiligt
choices.text_en: Module X likely participates in a cyclic dependency chain

choices.text: Modul X ist ein stabiles Kernmodul (Utility), das systemweit genutzt wird
choices.text_en: Module X is a stable core utility used across the system

choices.text: Modul X verletzt das Dependency-Inversion-Prinzip aufgrund seiner hohen Ce
choices.text_en: Module X violates the Dependency Inversion Principle due to its high Ce

text: Was ist ein ARCHITEKTUR-HOTSPOT?
text_en: What is an ARCHITECTURE HOTSPOT?
explanation: An architecture hotspot is a module or component that combines high change frequency (it is modified often), high coupling (many dependencies), and high complexity (each change is risky). The combination of all three factors makes it the most painful source of architectural debt — not any one factor alone.

choices.text: Ein Modul mit hoher Änderungshäufigkeit, hoher Kopplung UND hoher Komplexität — ein vorrangiger Risikobereich
choices.text_en: A module with high change frequency, high coupling, AND high complexity — a priority risk area

choices.text: Die performance-kritischste Komponente im System, gemessen unter Last
choices.text_en: The most performance-critical component in the system, as measured under load

choices.text: Jedes Modul, das querschnittliche Belange wie Logging oder Authentifizierung behandelt
choices.text_en: Any module that handles cross-cutting concerns such as logging or authentication

choices.text: Ein kürzlich hinzugefügtes Modul, das noch nicht vom Architekturteam überprüft wurde
choices.text_en: A recently added module that has not yet been reviewed by the architecture team

choices.text: Eine sicherheitskritische Komponente, die besondere Zugriffskontrollen und Auditing erfordert
choices.text_en: A security-critical component that requires special access controls and auditing

text: Wofür steht ATAM und was ist sein Zweck bei der Architekturbewertung?
text_en: What does ATAM stand for and what is its purpose in architecture evaluation?
explanation: ATAM = Architecture Tradeoff Analysis Method. It is a scenario-based evaluation method that systematically identifies trade-offs between quality attributes — for example, how a security decision (mandatory encryption) impacts performance. It reveals sensitivity points and trade-off points, helping stakeholders make informed architectural decisions.

choices.text: Architecture Tradeoff Analysis Method — identifiziert Trade-offs zwischen Qualitätsattributen
choices.text_en: Architecture Tradeoff Analysis Method — identifies trade-offs between quality attributes

choices.text: Automated Testing and Measurement — ein CI/CD-Pipeline-Strukturvalidierungsansatz
choices.text_en: Automated Testing and Measurement — a CI/CD pipeline structural validation approach

choices.text: Architecture Tactic Analysis Method — wählt systematisch Entwurfstaktiken pro Qualitätsziel aus
choices.text_en: Architecture Tactic Analysis Method — systematically selects design tactics per quality goal

choices.text: Agile Technical Assessment Model — eine leichtgewichtige Sprint-basierte Qualitätsüberprüfung
choices.text_en: Agile Technical Assessment Model — a lightweight sprint-based quality review

choices.text: Application Testing and Audit Method — ein automatisiertes Testkovervalidierungswerkzeug
choices.text_en: Application Testing and Audit Method — an automated test coverage validation tool

text: Welche ZWEI sind EINGABEN für eine ATAM-artige szenariobasierte Bewertung?
text_en: Which TWO are INPUTS to an ATAM-style scenario-based evaluation?
explanation: ATAM requires architectural documentation (views and decisions — to understand the system under evaluation) and quality attribute scenarios with stakeholder priorities (to drive the evaluation and identify trade-offs). Without the architecture to evaluate and scenarios to drive it, the process cannot function. Source code, database content, and project management artifacts are not required inputs.

choices.text: Architekturdokumentation (Sichten, Entscheidungen und Begründungen)
choices.text_en: Architecture documentation (views, decisions, and rationale)

choices.text: Qualitätsattribut-Szenarien mit Stakeholder-Prioritäten
choices.text_en: Quality attribute scenarios with stakeholder priorities

choices.text: Vollständiger Quellcode des zu bewertenden Systems
choices.text_en: Full source code of the system under evaluation

choices.text: Ein laufendes Deployment des Systems in einer Staging-Umgebung
choices.text_en: A working deployment of the system in a staging environment

choices.text: Der Projektplan und geschätzte Fertigstellungsmeilensteine
choices.text_en: The project plan and estimated completion milestones

text: Was ist das Stable-Dependencies-Prinzip (SDP) und warum ist es wichtig?
text_en: What is the Stable Dependencies Principle (SDP) and why does it matter?
explanation: SDP states that a component should only depend on components that are MORE stable than itself (i.e., the instability of the dependency should be <= the instability of the dependent). Violating SDP means depending on components that change frequently, which propagates instability through the system. This is separate from Dependency Injection, which is a DIP implementation technique.

choices.text: Nur von stabileren Modulen abhängen — verhindert die Ausbreitung von Instabilität in der Abhängigkeitskette
choices.text_en: Depend only on more stable modules — prevents instability from propagating up the dependency chain

choices.text: Alle Abhängigkeiten sollten injiziert statt konstruiert werden, um Testbarkeit zu ermöglichen
choices.text_en: All dependencies should be injected rather than constructed, to enable testing

choices.text: Nach der Freigabe sollten stabile Module unabhängig vom Geschäftsbedarf keine Änderungen akzeptieren
choices.text_en: Once released, stable modules should not accept changes regardless of business need

choices.text: Stabile Module sind als solche mit I=0,0 definiert und sollten niemals refaktoriert werden
choices.text_en: Stable modules are defined as those with I=0.0 and should never be refactored

choices.text: Nur Drittanbieter-Frameworks und -Bibliotheken sollten als stabile Abhängigkeiten behandelt werden
choices.text_en: Only third-party frameworks and libraries should be treated as stable dependencies

text: Welche Metrik wird verwendet, um ARCHITEKTUR-EROSION zu erkennen?
text_en: Which metric is used to detect ARCHITECTURE EROSION?
explanation: Architecture erosion is detected by comparing the intended architecture (documented design) to the implemented architecture (actual code). Conformance analysis tools automatically flag deviations — dependency rule violations, layer bypasses, and banned imports. Code coverage, commit counts, and sprint velocity are useful metrics but do not measure architectural conformance.

choices.text: Grad der Abweichung zwischen der dokumentierten und der implementierten Architektur (Konformität)
choices.text_en: Degree of deviation between the documented and implemented architecture (conformance)

choices.text: Anzahl der Commits pro Entwickler über ein 30-Tage-Fenster
choices.text_en: Number of commits per developer over a rolling 30-day window

choices.text: Zyklomatische Komplexität einzelner Funktionen in den kritischsten Modulen
choices.text_en: Cyclomatic complexity of individual functions in the most critical modules

choices.text: Testabdeckungsprozentsatz über alle Produktionsmodule
choices.text_en: Test coverage percentage across all production modules

choices.text: Sprint-Velocity im Vergleich zur historischen Baseline des Teams
choices.text_en: Sprint velocity compared to the team's historical baseline

text: Warum ist eine KONTINUIERLICHE Architekturbewertung (integriert in CI/CD) einer EINMALIGEN Bewertung an einem Projektmeilenstein vorzuziehen?
text_en: Why is CONTINUOUS architecture evaluation (integrated into CI/CD) preferable to ONE-TIME evaluation at a project milestone?
explanation: Architecture erodes incrementally as the system evolves through hundreds of small changes. A one-time evaluation captures only a snapshot at that moment. Continuous evaluation via fitness functions detects drift the moment a bad change is committed — keeping the cost of correction minimal and preventing accumulation of architectural debt.

choices.text: Erkennt Architektur-Drift sofort, wenn Änderungen eingecheckt werden, und verhindert die Ansammlung von Schulden
choices.text_en: Detects architecture drift immediately when changes are committed, preventing debt accumulation

choices.text: Kontinuierliche Bewertung ist vom iSAQB-Lehrplan für die CPSA-F-Zertifizierung vorgeschrieben
choices.text_en: Continuous evaluation is mandated by the iSAQB curriculum for CPSA-F certification

choices.text: Einmalige Bewertungen sind zu teuer und werden vom Management selten genehmigt
choices.text_en: One-time evaluations are prohibitively expensive and rarely approved by management

choices.text: Kontinuierliche Bewertung eliminiert den Bedarf an regelmäßigen Experten-Architekturreviews
choices.text_en: Continuous evaluation eliminates the need for periodic expert architecture reviews

choices.text: Kontinuierliche Bewertung ist der einzige Ansatz, der von Microservices-Architekturen unterstützt wird
choices.text_en: Continuous evaluation is the only approach supported by microservices architectures

text: Ein Team vermutet, dass ihre Domainservice-Schicht zur 'God Class' wird. Welche Metrikkombination BESTÄTIGT diesen Verdacht am besten?
text_en: A team suspects their domain service layer is becoming a god class. Which metric combination BEST confirms this suspicion?
explanation: A god class shows very high efferent coupling (Ce — depends on many things), often accompanied by high afferent coupling (Ca — many depend on it), and high cyclomatic complexity (each change to it is risky and error-prone). This combination signals a class that has accumulated too many responsibilities. High cohesion and low coupling would indicate the opposite — a well-designed class.

choices.text: Hohe Ce (viele ausgehende Abhängigkeiten) kombiniert mit hoher zyklomatischer Komplexität
choices.text_en: High Ce (many outgoing dependencies) combined with high cyclomatic complexity

choices.text: Niedrige Ca (wenige Aufrufer) und niedrige Ce (wenige Abhängigkeiten) — unterverbundenes Modul
choices.text_en: Low Ca (few callers) and low Ce (few dependencies) — under-connected module

choices.text: I=0,0 (maximal stabil) mit hoher Unit-Test-Abdeckung
choices.text_en: I=0.0 (maximally stable) with high unit test coverage

choices.text: Hohe Kohäsion und niedrige Kopplung — deutet auf klare Einzelverantwortung hin
choices.text_en: High cohesion and low coupling — indicates clear single responsibility

choices.text: Niedrige zyklomatische Komplexität kombiniert mit einer hohen Anzahl von Aufrufern
choices.text_en: Low cyclomatic complexity combined with a large number of callers

text: Welche ZWEI Werkzeuge können KEINE ZYKLISCHEN ABHÄNGIGKEITEN als Fitness-Funktion in einer CI-Pipeline durchsetzen?
text_en: Which TWO tools can enforce NO CYCLIC DEPENDENCIES as a fitness function in a CI pipeline?
explanation: ArchUnit (Java) and Dependency Cruiser (JS/TS) are tools that run automated architectural structure checks in CI, including cycle detection. They integrate into the build pipeline and fail the build when a violation is introduced. SonarQube detects code smells and bugs but does not specialize in dependency cycle enforcement as a fitness function.

choices.text: ArchUnit (Java) — setzt Architekturregeln als ausführbare Tests durch
choices.text_en: ArchUnit (Java) — enforces architectural rules as executable tests

choices.text: Dependency Cruiser (JS/TS) — validiert Abhängigkeitsregeln und erkennt Zyklen in CI
choices.text_en: Dependency Cruiser (JS/TS) — validates dependency rules and detects cycles in CI

choices.text: SonarQube — erkennt Code Smells, Fehler und Sicherheitslücken
choices.text_en: SonarQube — detects code smells, bugs, and security vulnerabilities

choices.text: JUnit — Unit-Tests, die das Verhalten einzelner Komponenten überprüfen
choices.text_en: JUnit — unit tests that verify the behavior of individual components

choices.text: Postman — API-Vertragstests, die Request/Response-Schemata validieren
choices.text_en: Postman — API contract tests that validate request/response schemas

text: Was ist ein QUALITÄTSATTRIBUT-SZENARIO (im Kontext der Architekturbewertung)?
text_en: What is a QUALITY ATTRIBUTE SCENARIO (in the context of architecture evaluation)?
explanation: A quality attribute scenario is a concrete, testable description of how the system must behave under a specific quality condition. It includes: stimulus (what happens), environment (context), response (how the system reacts), and response measure (the quantifiable criterion for success). This structure makes quality requirements evaluatable, unlike vague statements like 'the system should be fast.'

choices.text: Eine konkrete, testbare Beschreibung mit Stimulus, Umgebung, Reaktion und Messmaßstab
choices.text_en: A concrete, testable description with stimulus, environment, response, and response measure

choices.text_en: A user story that captures a non-functional requirement in the product backlog

choices.text: Ein UML-Use-Case-Diagramm, erweitert um Qualitätsrestriktionen
choices.text_en: A UML use case diagram extended with quality constraints

choices.text: Ein Stakeholder-Interview-Protokoll, das Qualitätsbedenken dokumentiert
choices.text_en: A stakeholder interview transcript documenting quality concerns

choices.text: Ein Eintrag im Architekturrisiko-Register, der einen möglichen Qualitätsfehler beschreibt
choices.text_en: A risk entry in the architecture risk register describing a potential quality failure

text: Was legt der REAKTIONS-MESSMASSTAB (Response Measure) in einem Qualitätsattribut-Szenario fest?
text_en: In a quality attribute scenario, what does the RESPONSE MEASURE specify?
explanation: The response measure makes a scenario evaluatable by defining a quantifiable criterion for success. Without it, the scenario remains a vague aspiration. For example, 'responds quickly' is not a response measure; '99% of requests complete within 200ms under 1000 concurrent users' is.

choices.text: Ein messbares Kriterium, das bestimmt, ob die Systemreaktion akzeptabel ist
choices.text_en: A measurable criterion that determines whether the system's response is acceptable

choices.text: Das Observability-Werkzeug (APM, Tracing), das zur Messung des Systems in Produktion eingesetzt wird

choices.text_en: The test framework and tooling used to execute the scenario in CI

choices.text_en: The component or team responsible for delivering the quality attribute

choices.text: Das von Stakeholdern zugewiesene Geschäftspriorität-Gewicht dieses Qualitätsattributs
choices.text_en: The business priority weight assigned to this quality attribute by stakeholders

text: Welches der folgenden ist ein wohlgeformtes QUALITÄTSATTRIBUT-SZENARIO?
text_en: Which of the following is a well-formed QUALITY ATTRIBUTE SCENARIO?
explanation: A well-formed scenario is concrete and measurable: it specifies the stimulus (1000 concurrent users at checkout), the environment (peak load), the response (checkout API processes the request), and the quantifiable response measure (responds in 200ms for 99% of requests). Vague statements like 'the system must be fast' lack a response measure and cannot be evaluated.

choices.text: Bei Spitzenlast von 1000 gleichzeitigen Benutzern antwortet die Checkout-API für 99 % der Anfragen innerhalb von 200 ms
choices.text_en: Under peak load of 1000 concurrent users, the checkout API responds within 200ms for 99% of requests

choices.text: Das System muss schnell und reaktionsschnell sein, um eine gute Benutzererfahrung zu gewährleisten
choices.text_en: The system must be fast and responsive to ensure a good user experience

choices.text: Benutzer sollten unter normalen Betriebsbedingungen mit den Antwortzeiten zufrieden sein
choices.text_en: Users should be satisfied with the response times under normal operating conditions

choices.text: Performance ist ein hoch priorisiertes Qualitätsattribut, das in der Architektur berücksichtigt werden muss
choices.text_en: Performance is a high-priority quality attribute that must be addressed in the architecture

choices.text: Die API-Schicht muss entworfen und optimiert werden, um hochfrequentierte Szenarien effizient zu handhaben
choices.text_en: The API layer must be designed and optimized to handle high-traffic scenarios efficiently

text: Warum ist HOHE AFFERENTE KOPPLUNG (Ca) nicht automatisch ein Problem?
text_en: Why is HIGH AFFERENT COUPLING (Ca) not automatically a problem?
explanation: High Ca means many modules depend on this one — this is expected and appropriate for stable utility/core modules (a logging library, a domain value object). The problem arises when a highly-used module (high Ca) is also unstable (high Ce, changes frequently). Metrics must always be interpreted in the context of the module's intended role.

choices.text: Hohe Ca ist für stabile Hilfsmodule (Utilities) erwartet und korrekt, auf die viele andere legitimerweise zugreifen
choices.text_en: High Ca is expected and correct for stable utility modules that many others legitimately reuse

choices.text_en: High Ca always signals high cohesion, which is a desirable design property

choices.text: Hohe Ca ist nur in Legacy-Systemen ein Problem, nicht in modernen Greenfield-Microservices-Architekturen
choices.text_en: High Ca is only a problem in legacy systems, not in greenfield microservice architectures

choices.text: Hohe Ca bedeutet, dass das Modul vom Rest des Systems isoliert und in sich geschlossen ist

text: Klassifizieren Sie jede Architekturbewertungstechnik als QUALITATIV oder QUANTITATIV.
text_en: Classify each architecture evaluation technique as QUALITATIVE or QUANTITATIVE.
explanation: Qualitative methods produce judgments based on expertise and reasoning: expert reviews and ATAM scenario walkthroughs. Quantitative methods produce numeric measurements: coupling metric computation, automated latency measurements, and cycle counts via tooling. The distinction matters because the two types complement each other — metrics surface signals, experts interpret them.

choices.text_en: ATAM scenario-based evaluation

choices.text: Berechnung von afferenten/efferenten Kopplungsmetriken
choices.text_en: Afferent/efferent coupling metric computation

choices.text: Automatisierte Latenz-P99-Messung
choices.text_en: Automated latency P99 measurement
